Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.\n53\ttype ManifestRecorder interface {\n54\t\t// RecordPut records a successful PutObject or CompleteMultipartUpload.\n55\t\tRecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string)\n56\t\t// RecordDelete records a successful DeleteObject.\n57\t\tRecordDelete(bucket, key string)\n58\t\t// Lookup returns manifest metadata for bucket/key, or (nil, false) if not\n59\t\t// tracked. != \"\" {\n224\t\t\t\th.CreateBucket(w, r, bucket)\n225\t\t\t}\n226\t\tcase http.MethodHead:\n227\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n228\t\t\t\th.HeadObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n229\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n230\t\t\t\th.HeadBucket(w, r, bucket)\n231\t\t\t}\n232\t\tcase http.MethodDelete:\n233\t\t\t// Handle DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration (DELETE ?lifecycle on bucket)\n234\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"lifecycle\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n235\t\t\t\th.DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n236\t\t\t\treturn\n237\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\t// Handle AbortMultipartUpload (DELETE ?uploadId on object)\n239\t\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && key != \"\" {\n240\t\t\t\th.AbortMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n241\t\t\t\treturn\n242\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n244\t\t\t\th.DeleteObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n245\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n246\t\t\t\th.DeleteBucket(w, r, bucket)\n247\t\t\t}\n248\t\tcase http.MethodPost:\n249\t\t\t// Handle multipart upload operations\n250\t\t\tuploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\")\n251\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"uploads\") {\n252\t\t\t\th.CreateMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key)\n253\t\t\t} else if uploadID != \"\" {\n254\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n255\t\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n256\t\t\t\t} else {\n257\t\t\t\t\th.CompleteMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n258\t\t\t\t}\n259\t\t\t} else if r.URL.Query().Has(\"delete\") {\n260\t\t\t\t// DeleteObjects (bulk delete) - uses POST with ?delete query param\n261\t\t\t\th.DeleteObjects(w, r, bucket)\n262\t\t\t} else {\n263\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Unsupported POST operation\", 400)\n264\t\t\t}\n265\t\tdefault:\n266\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MethodNotAllowed\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Method %s not allowed\", r.Method), 405)\n267\t\t}\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// PutObject handles S3 PutObject with encryption.\n271\t// For small files (<10MB), it buffers in memory.\n272\t// For larger files, it uses streaming encryption via temp files to avoid\n273\t// loading the entire file into memory.\n274\tfunc (h *Handlers) PutObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n275\t\tctx := r.Context()\n276\t\n277\t\t// Check Content-Length header\n278\t\tcontentLength := r.ContentLength\n279\t\tstreamingThreshold := int64(10 * 1024 * 1024) // 10MB threshold\n280\t\n281\t\t// Use streaming for large files or unknown size\n282\t\tuseStreaming := contentLength < 0 || contentLength > streamingThreshold\n283\t\n284\t\tif useStreaming {\n285\t\t\th.putObjectStreaming(ctx, w, r, bucket, key)\n286\t\t\treturn\n287\t\t}\n288\t\n289\t\t// Small file: buffer in memory\n290\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n291\t\tif err != nil {\n292\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n293\t\t\treturn\n294\t\t}\n295\t\n296\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n297\t\n298\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n299\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n300\t\tif err != nil {\n301\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n302\t\t\treturn\n303\t\t}\n304\t\n305\t\t// Generate DEK and IV\n306\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n307\t\tif err != nil {\n308\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n309\t\t\treturn\n310\t\t}\n311\t\n312\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n313\t\tif err != nil {\n314\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n315\t\t\treturn\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n319\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n320\t\tif err != nil {\n321\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n322\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t}\n324\t\n325\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n326\t\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(plaintext)\n327\t\n328\t\t// Create envelope header\n329\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n330\t\tif err != nil {\n331\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n332\t\t\treturn\n333\t\t}\n334\t\n335\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n336\t\tif err != nil {\n337\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n338\t\t\treturn\n339\t\t}\n340\t\n341\t\t// Encrypt data\n342\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n343\t\tif err != nil {\n344\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n345\t\t\treturn\n346\t\t}\n347\t\n348\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n349\t\tif err != nil {\n350\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n351\t\t\treturn\n352\t\t}\n353\t\n354\t\t// Build envelope: header + encrypted blocks + HMAC table\n355\t\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + int64(len(encrypted)) + int64(len(hmacTable))\n356\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, envelopeSize)\n357\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n358\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n359\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n360\t\n361\t\t// Compute plaintext ETag (MD5)\n362\t\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(plaintext)\n363\t\n364\t\t// Build metadata\n365\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n366\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n367\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n368\t\t}\n369\t\n370\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n371\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n372\t\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n373\t\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n374\t\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n375\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n376\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n377\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n378\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n379\t\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n380\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n381\t\n382\t\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied\n383\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n384\t\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n385\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n386\t\t\treturn\n387\t\t}\n388\t\n389\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n390\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n391\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n392\t\t}\n393\t\n394\t\t// Record provenance\n395\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n396\t\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n397\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put\")\n398\t\t}\n399\t\n400\t\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n401\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n402\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n403\t\t}\n404\t\n405\t\t// Return ETag\n406\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n407\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n408\t}\n409\t\n410\t// putObjectStreaming handles large file uploads with streaming encryption.\n411\t// It uses a temp file to avoid loading the entire plaintext into memory.\n412\t// The process is:\n413\t// 1. Stream request body to temp file while computing SHA-256\n414\t// 2. Create envelope header with the computed SHA-256\n415\t// 3. Stream from temp file through encryption to B2 via io.Pipe\n416\t// 4. Clean up temp file\n417\tfunc (h *Handlers) putObjectStreaming(ctx context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n418\t\t// Phase 1: Stream to temp file and compute SHA-256\n419\t\ttmpFile, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-upload-*.tmp\")\n420\t\tif err != nil {\n421\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n422\t\t\treturn\n423\t\t}\n424\t\ttmpPath := tmpFile.Name()\n425\t\tdefer os.Remove(tmpPath) // Clean up on exit\n426\t\n427\t\t// Compute SHA-256 while copying to temp file\n428\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n429\t\tteeReader := io.TeeReader(r.Body, plaintextHash)\n430\t\n431\t\tplaintextSize, err := io.Copy(tmpFile, teeReader)\n432\t\tif err != nil {\n433\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n434\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n435\t\t\treturn\n436\t\t}\n437\t\n438\t\t// Get the computed SHA-256\n439\t\tvar plaintextSHA [32]byte\n440\t\tcopy(plaintextSHA[:], plaintextHash.Sum(nil))\n441\t\n442\t\t// Seek back to beginning of temp file for reading\n443\t\tif _, err := tmpFile.Seek(0, 0); err != nil {\n444\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n445\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to seek temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n446\t\t\treturn\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\t// Phase 2: Get encryption keys\n450\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n451\t\tif err != nil {\n452\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n453\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n454\t\t\treturn\n455\t\t}\n456\t\n457\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n458\t\tif err != nil {\n459\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n460\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n461\t\t\treturn\n462\t\t}\n463\t\n464\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n465\t\tif err != nil {\n466\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n467\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n468\t\t\treturn\n469\t\t}\n470\t\n471\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n472\t\tif err != nil {\n473\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n474\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n475\t\t\treturn\n476\t\t}\n477\t\n478\t\t// Create envelope header\n479\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n480\t\tif err != nil {\n481\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n482\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n483\t\t\treturn\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n487\t\tif err != nil {\n488\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n489\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n490\t\t\treturn\n491\t\t}\n492\t\n493\t\t// Create encryptor\n494\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n495\t\tif err != nil {\n496\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n497\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n498\t\t\treturn\n499\t\t}\n500\t\n501\t\t// Calculate envelope size\n502\t\tblockCount := crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize)\n503\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n504\t\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + plaintextSize + hmacTableSize\n505\t\n506\t\t// Phase 3: Stream encrypt via io.Pipe\n507\t\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n508\t\n509\t\t// Start encryption goroutine\n510\t\tencErr := make(chan error, 1)\n511\t\tgo func() {\n512\t\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n513\t\t\tdefer close(encErr)\n514\t\n515\t\t\t// Write header\n516\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(headerBytes); err != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write header: %w\", err)\n518\t\t\t\treturn\n519\t\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t\t// Stream encrypt the plaintext\n522\t\t\thmacTable, err := encryptor.EncryptStream(tmpFile, pw, plaintextSize)\n523\t\t\tif err != nil {\n524\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"encryption failed: %w\", err)\n525\t\t\t\treturn\n526\t\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t\t// Write HMAC table\n529\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(hmacTable); err != nil {\n530\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write HMAC table: %w\", err)\n531\t\t\t\treturn\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\n534\t\t\tencErr <- nil\n535\t\t}()\n536\t\n537\t\t// Build metadata\n538\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n539\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n540\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n541\t\t}\n542\t\n543\t\t// Compute ETag while we have the temp file\n544\t\t// We need to read the file again for MD5, but we already have SHA-256\n545\t\t// Use SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes as ETag for streaming (non-standard but works)\n546\t\tetag := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:16])\n547\t\n548\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n549\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n550\t\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n551\t\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n552\t\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n553\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n554\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n555\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n556\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n557\t\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n558\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n559\t\n560\t\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied using streaming reader\n561\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n562\t\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, pr, envelopeSize, meta); err != nil {\n563\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n564\t\t\t// Check if there was an encryption error\n565\t\t\tselect {\n566\t\t\tcase encErrVal := <-encErr:\n567\t\t\t\tif encErrVal != nil {\n568\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n569\t\t\t\t\treturn\n570\t\t\t\t}\n571\t\t\tdefault:\n572\t\t\t}\n573\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n574\t\t\treturn\n575\t\t}\n576\t\n577\t\t// Close temp file\n578\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n579\t\n580\t\t// Check for encryption errors\n581\t\tif encErrVal := <-encErr; encErrVal != nil {\n582\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n583\t\t\treturn\n584\t\t}\n585\t\n586\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n587\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n588\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n589\t\t}\n590\t\n591\t\t// Record provenance\n592\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n593\t\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n594\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put-streaming\")\n595\t\t}\n596\t\n597\t\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n598\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n599\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Return ETag\n603\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n604\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Streaming\", \"true\")\n605\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n606\t}\n607\t\n608\t// GetObject handles S3 GetObject with decryption and range support.\n609\tfunc (h *Handlers) GetObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n610\t\tctx := r.Context()\n611\t\n612\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n613\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n614\t\n615\t\t// Get metadata first\n616\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n617\t\tif err != nil {\n618\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n619\t\t\treturn\n620\t\t}\n621\t\n622\t\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n623\t\t\t// Check conditional request headers for non-ARMOR objects\n624\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, info.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n625\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n626\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n627\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n628\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n629\t\t\t\t} else {\n630\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n631\t\t\t\t}\n632\t\t\t\treturn\n633\t\t\t}\n634\t\n635\t\t\t// Passthrough for non-ARMOR objects\n636\t\t\tbody, _, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n637\t\t\tif err != nil {\n638\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), 500)\n639\t\t\t\treturn\n640\t\t\t}\n641\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n642\t\n643\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(info.Size, 10))\n644\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n645\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n646\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n647\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n648\t\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n649\t\t\treturn\n650\t\t}\n651\t\n652\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n653\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n654\t\tif !ok {\n655\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n656\t\t\treturn\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\t// Get the MEK for this object using the key ID from metadata\n660\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n661\t\tif err != nil {\n662\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n663\t\t\treturn\n664\t\t}\n665\t\n666\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n667\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n668\t\tif err != nil {\n669\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n670\t\t\treturn\n671\t\t}\n672\t\n673\t\t// Create decryptor\n674\t\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n675\t\tif err != nil {\n676\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create decryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n677\t\t\treturn\n678\t\t}\n679\t\n680\t\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n681\t\n682\t\t// Check conditional request headers\n683\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, armorMeta.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n684\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n685\t\t\t\t// 304 Not Modified - set headers but no body\n686\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n687\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n688\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n689\t\t\t} else {\n690\t\t\t\t// 412 Precondition Failed\n691\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n692\t\t\t}\n693\t\t\treturn\n694\t\t}\n695\t\n696\t\t// Check for range request\n697\t\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n698\t\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n699\t\t\th.handleRangeRequest(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified)\n700\t\t\treturn\n701\t\t}\n702\t\n703\t\t// Full object download with pipelined stream decryption\n704\t\th.handleFullObjectStream(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified)\n705\t}\n706\t\n707\t// handleFullObjectStream handles full object downloads with pipelined stream decryption.\n708\t// This uses io.Pipe to decrypt blocks as they stream from Cloudflare, reducing\n709\t// time-to-first-byte and memory usage compared to buffering the entire envelope.\n710\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleFullObjectStream(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time) {\n711\t\tctx := r.Context()\n712\t\n713\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n714\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n715\t\n716\t\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n717\t\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\n718\t\n719\t\t// Calculate offsets\n720\t\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n721\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n722\t\tdataSize := plaintextSize\n723\t\n724\t\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table (small range read)\n725\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n726\t\tif err != nil {\n727\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n728\t\t\treturn\n729\t\t}\n730\t\thmacTable, err := io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n731\t\thmacBody.Close()\n732\t\tif err != nil {\n733\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n734\t\t\treturn\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\t// 2. Start streaming data from Cloudflare (header + encrypted blocks, stop before HMAC)\n738\t\tstreamSize := crypto.HeaderSize + dataSize\n739\t\tdataBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\n740\t\tif err != nil {\n741\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), 500)\n742\t\t\treturn\n743\t\t}\n744\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n745\t\n746\t\t// 3. Read and discard the 64-byte header\n747\t\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n748\t\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n749\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), 500)\n750\t\t\treturn\n751\t\t}\n752\t\n753\t\t// Parse header to get plaintext SHA for verification\n754\t\theader, err := crypto.DecodeHeader(headerBuf)\n755\t\tif err != nil {\n756\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode header: %v\", err), 500)\n757\t\t\treturn\n758\t\t}\n759\t\n760\t\t// 4. Set response headers before streaming\n761\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(plaintextSize, 10))\n762\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n763\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n764\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n765\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n766\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Stream\", \"pipelined\")\n767\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n768\t\n769\t\t// 5. Stream decrypt using io.Pipe\n770\t\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n771\t\n772\t\t// Start decryption goroutine\n773\t\tgo func() {\n774\t\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n775\t\n776\t\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n777\t\t\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n778\t\n779\t\t\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n780\t\t\t\t// Calculate actual block size (last block may be smaller)\n781\t\t\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n782\t\t\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n783\t\n784\t\t\t\t// Read encrypted block\n785\t\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n786\t\t\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n787\t\t\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n788\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"read error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n789\t\t\t\t\treturn\n790\t\t\t\t}\n791\t\t\t\tif n == 0 {\n792\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n793\t\t\t\t}\n794\t\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n795\t\n796\t\t\t\t// Verify HMAC\n797\t\t\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n798\t\t\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n799\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC table too short at block %d\", blockIndex))\n800\t\t\t\t\treturn\n801\t\t\t\t}\n802\t\t\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n803\t\n804\t\t\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n805\t\t\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n806\t\t\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n807\t\t\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n808\t\t\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n809\t\t\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n810\t\n811\t\t\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n812\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"block %d: HMAC verification failed\", blockIndex))\n813\t\t\t\t\treturn\n814\t\t\t\t}\n815\t\n816\t\t\t\t// Decrypt block (need to use CTR stream)\n817\t\t\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n818\t\t\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n819\t\t\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n820\t\t\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n821\t\n822\t\t\t\t// Update plaintext hash for verification\n823\t\t\t\tplaintextHash.Write(decrypted)\n824\t\n825\t\t\t\t// Write plaintext to pipe\n826\t\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(decrypted); err != nil {\n827\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"write error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n828\t\t\t\t\treturn\n829\t\t\t\t}\n830\t\t\t}\n831\t\n832\t\t\t// Verify plaintext SHA-256\n833\t\t\tcomputedSHA := plaintextHash.Sum(nil)\n834\t\t\tif !bytes.Equal(computedSHA, header.PlaintextSHA[:]) {\n835\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA-256 mismatch\"))\n836\t\t\t\treturn\n837\t\t\t}\n838\t\t}()\n839\t\n840\t\t// Stream plaintext to client\n841\t\tio.Copy(w, pr)\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\t// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\n845\tfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n846\t\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n847\t\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n848\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n849\t\treturn counter\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\tfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n853\t\tif a < b {\n854\t\t\treturn a\n855\t\t}\n856\t\treturn b\n857\t}\n858\t\n859\t// handleRangeRequest handles range read requests.\n860\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time) {\n861\t\tctx := r.Context()\n862\t\n863\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n864\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n865\t\n866\t\t// Parse range header (bytes=start-end)\n867\t\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n868\t\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n869\t\tif err != nil {\n870\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRange\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid range: %v\", err), 400)\n871\t\t\treturn\n872\t\t}\n873\t\n874\t\t// Check if this is a Parquet footer request and we have it cached\n875\t\t// DuckDB reads footer in two steps: last 8 bytes, then footer body\n876\t\t// Footer is at the end of the file: [footer_metadata][footer_length (4B)][PAR1 (4B)]\n877\t\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 {\n878\t\t\t// This range includes the end of the file - could be a footer read\n879\t\t\tif footer, ok := h.footerCache.Get(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag); ok {\n880\t\t\t\t// We have a cached footer, serve from cache\n881\t\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(footer))\n882\t\t\t\tif start >= footerStart {\n883\t\t\t\t\t// Request is entirely within the cached footer\n884\t\t\t\t\toffset := start - footerStart\n885\t\t\t\t\tfooterEnd := offset + (end - start + 1)\n886\t\t\t\t\tif footerEnd <= int64(len(footer)) {\n887\t\t\t\t\t\tplaintext := footer[offset:footerEnd]\n888\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n889\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n890\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n891\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n892\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n893\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Footer-Cache\", \"HIT\")\n894\t\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n895\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n896\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n897\t\t\t\t\t}\n898\t\t\t\t}\n899\t\t\t}\n900\t\t}\n901\t\n902\t\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n903\t\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n904\t\tif err != nil {\n905\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), 500)\n906\t\t\treturn\n907\t\t}\n908\t\n909\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel using errgroup.\n910\t\t// This cuts range-read latency nearly in half for cache misses.\n911\t\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n912\t\n913\t\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n914\t\n915\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n916\t\t\tencryptedBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n917\t\t\tif err != nil {\n918\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n919\t\t\t}\n920\t\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n921\t\n922\t\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n923\t\t\tif err != nil {\n924\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n925\t\t\t}\n926\t\t\treturn nil\n927\t\t})\n928\t\n929\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n930\t\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n931\t\t\tif err != nil {\n932\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n933\t\t\t}\n934\t\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n935\t\n936\t\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n937\t\t\tif err != nil {\n938\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n939\t\t\t}\n940\t\t\treturn nil\n941\t\t})\n942\t\n943\t\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n944\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", err.Error(), 500)\n945\t\t\treturn\n946\t\t}\n947\t\n948\t\t// Decrypt range\n949\t\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize)\n950\t\tif err != nil {\n951\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), 500)\n952\t\t\treturn\n953\t\t}\n954\t\n955\t\t// Cache Parquet footer if this looks like a footer read\n956\t\t// Footer is detected by: 1) Reading near end of file, 2) Data ends with \"PAR1\" magic\n957\t\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 && len(plaintext) >= 8 {\n958\t\t\tif backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n959\t\t\t\t// This is a Parquet file, try to cache the full footer\n960\t\t\t\t// If we just read the last 8 bytes, cache it for footer length detection\n961\t\t\t\t// If we read more, it might be the full footer\n962\t\t\t\th.cacheParquetFooter(ctx, bucket, key, armorMeta, plaintext, plaintextSize)\n963\t\t\t}\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\t// Set response headers\n967\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n968\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n969\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n970\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n971\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n972\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n973\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n974\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n975\t}\n976\t\n977\t// cacheParquetFooter caches Parquet footer data for faster subsequent reads.\n978\tfunc (h *Handlers) cacheParquetFooter(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintext []byte, plaintextSize int64) {\n979\t\t// If we got the last 8 bytes, we can determine footer length\n980\t\tif len(plaintext) == 8 {\n981\t\t\t// This is the footer length read - cache a small marker\n982\t\t\t// The actual footer will be cached on the next read\n983\t\t\treturn\n984\t\t}\n985\t\n986\t\t// Check if we have the full footer by verifying PAR1 magic at end\n987\t\tif len(plaintext) >= 8 && backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n988\t\t\t// We have at least part of the footer\n989\t\t\t// Parse footer length from the last 8 bytes if available\n990\t\t\tfooterRange, err := backend.GetParquetFooterRange(plaintext[len(plaintext)-8:], plaintextSize)\n991\t\t\tif err != nil {\n992\t\t\t\treturn\n993\t\t\t}\n994\t\n995\t\t\t// Check if we have the complete footer\n996\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(footerRange.Length) - 8\n997\t\t\trequestStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(plaintext))\n998\t\n999\t\t\tif requestStart <= footerStart {\n1000\t\t\t\t// We have the complete footer, extract and cache it\n1001\t\t\t\tfooterOffset := footerStart - requestStart\n1002\t\t\t\tif footerOffset >= 0 && int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8 <= len(plaintext) {\n1003\t\t\t\t\tfooter := plaintext[footerOffset : int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8]\n1004\t\t\t\t\th.footerCache.Set(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag, footer)\n1005\t\t\t\t}\n1006\t\t\t}\n1007\t\t}\n1008\t}\n1009\t\n1010\t// checkConditionalRequest evaluates conditional headers and returns the appropriate\n1011\t// response status. Returns 0 if the request should proceed normally.\n1012\t// Supports: If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\n1013\tfunc checkConditionalRequest(r *http.Request, etag string, lastModified time.Time) int {\n1014\t\tifMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-Match\")\n1015\t\tifNoneMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-None-Match\")\n1016\t\tifModifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Modified-Since\")\n1017\t\tifUnmodifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Unmodified-Since\")\n1018\t\n1019\t\t// Normalize ETag (remove quotes if present for comparison)\n1020\t\tnormalizedETag := strings.Trim(etag, `\"`)\n1021\t\n1022\t\t// If-Match: Return 412 Precondition Failed if ETag doesn't match\n1023\t\tif ifMatch != \"\" {\n1024\t\t\t// If-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n1025\t\t\tif ifMatch == \"*\" {\n1026\t\t\t\t// Match any existing resource - proceed\n1027\t\t\t} else {\n1028\t\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n1029\t\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifMatch, \",\")\n1030\t\t\t\tmatched := false\n1031\t\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n1032\t\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n1033\t\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n1034\t\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n1035\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n1036\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1037\t\t\t\t\t}\n1038\t\t\t\t}\n1039\t\t\t\tif !matched {\n1040\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n1041\t\t\t\t}\n1042\t\t\t}\n1043\t\t}\n1044\t\n1045\t\t// If-Unmodified-Since: Return 412 Precondition Failed if modified since date\n1046\t\tif ifUnmodifiedSince != \"\" {\n1047\t\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifUnmodifiedSince); err == nil {\n1048\t\t\t\tif lastModified.After(t) {\n1049\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n1050\t\t\t\t}\n1051\t\t\t}\n1052\t\t}\n1053\t\n1054\t\t// If-None-Match: Return 304 Not Modified if ETag matches\n1055\t\tif ifNoneMatch != \"\" {\n1056\t\t\t// If-None-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n1057\t\t\tif ifNoneMatch == \"*\" {\n1058\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1059\t\t\t}\n1060\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n1061\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifNoneMatch, \",\")\n1062\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n1063\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n1064\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n1065\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n1066\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1067\t\t\t\t}\n1068\t\t\t}\n1069\t\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t\t// If-Modified-Since: Return 304 Not Modified if not modified since date\n1072\t\t// Only applies if If-None-Match is not present (per RFC 7232)\n1073\t\tif ifModifiedSince != \"\" && ifNoneMatch == \"\" {\n1074\t\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifModifiedSince); err == nil {\n1075\t\t\t\t// Use >= comparison per RFC 7232\n1076\t\t\t\tif !lastModified.After(t) {\n1077\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1078\t\t\t\t}\n1079\t\t\t}\n1080\t\t}\n1081\t\n1082\t\treturn 0\n1083\t}\n1084\t\n1085\t// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\n1086\tfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n1087\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n1088\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1089\t\t}\n1090\t\n1091\t\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n1092\t\n1093\t\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n1094\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n1095\t\t}\n1096\t\n1097\t\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n1098\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n1099\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1100\t\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n1103\t\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n1104\t\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n1105\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1106\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1107\t\t\t}\n1108\t\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n1109\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1110\t\t} else {\n1111\t\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n1112\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1113\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1114\t\t\t}\n1115\t\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n1116\t\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1117\t\t\t} else {\n1118\t\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n1119\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1120\t\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1121\t\t\t\t}\n1122\t\t\t}\n1123\t\t}\n1124\t\n1125\t\t// Clamp end per RFC 7233: a range ending beyond the file size is satisfiable\n1126\t\t// as long as start is within bounds. DuckDB httpfs requests aligned blocks\n1127\t\t// (e.g. bytes=0-131071) regardless of actual file size.\n1128\t\tif end >= totalSize {\n1129\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1130\t\t}\n1131\t\n1132\t\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start {\n1133\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n1134\t\t}\n1135\t\n1136\t\treturn start, end, nil\n1137\t}\n1138\t\n1139\t// HeadObject handles S3 HeadObject.\n1140\tfunc (h *Handlers) HeadObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1141\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1142\t\n1143\t\t// Fast path: serve from the in-memory manifest index when available,\n1144\t\t// avoiding a B2 HeadObject round-trip entirely.\n1145\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1146\t\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, key); ok {\n1147\t\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, entry.ETag, entry.LastModified); status != 0 {\n1148\t\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n1149\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n1150\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1151\t\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n1152\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n1153\t\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n1154\t\t\t\t\t}\n1155\t\t\t\t\treturn\n1156\t\t\t\t}\n1157\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(entry.PlaintextSize, 10))\n1158\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", entry.ContentType)\n1159\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n1160\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1161\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1162\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1163\t\t\t\treturn\n1164\t\t\t}\n1165\t\t}\n1166\t\n1167\t\t// Manifest miss or disabled: fall back to a B2 HeadObject call.\n1168\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n1169\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n1170\t\tif err != nil {\n1171\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Object not found\", 404)\n1172\t\t\treturn\n1173\t\t}\n1174\t\n1175\t\t// Determine ETag and content info based on encryption status\n1176\t\tvar etag string\n1177\t\tvar contentLength int64\n1178\t\tvar contentType string\n1179\t\tif info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n1180\t\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n1181\t\t\t\tetag = am.ETag\n1182\t\t\t\tcontentLength = am.PlaintextSize\n1183\t\t\t\tcontentType = am.ContentType\n1184\t\t\t} else {\n1185\t\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n1186\t\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n1187\t\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n1188\t\t\t}\n1189\t\t} else {\n1190\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n1191\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n1192\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n1193\t\t}\n1194\t\n1195\t\t// Check conditional request headers\n1196\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, etag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n1197\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n1198\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1199\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1200\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n1201\t\t\t} else {\n1202\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n1203\t\t\t}\n1204\t\t\treturn\n1205\t\t}\n1206\t\n1207\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(contentLength, 10))\n1208\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", contentType)\n1209\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1210\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1211\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1212\t\n1213\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1214\t}\n1215\t\n1216\t// DeleteObject handles S3 DeleteObject.\n1217\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1218\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1219\t\n1220\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n1221\t\tif err := h.backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey); err != nil {\n1222\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to delete: %v\", err), 500)\n1223\t\t\treturn\n1224\t\t}\n1225\t\n1226\t\t// Remove from manifest\n1227\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1228\t\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n1229\t\t}\n1230\t\n1231\t\t// Invalidate metadata cache and list cache\n1232\t\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n1233\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1234\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n1235\t\t}\n1236\t\n1237\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n1238\t}\n1239\t\n1240\t// CopyObject handles S3 CopyObject with DEK re-wrapping for ARMOR-encrypted objects.\n1241\t// This supports:\n1242\t// - Renaming files (same bucket, different key)\n1243\t// - Copying files (potentially different bucket)\n1244\t// - Key rotation (re-wraps DEK with current MEK)\n1245\tfunc (h *Handlers) CopyObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dstBucket, dstKey string) {\n1246\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1247\t\n1248\t\t// Parse copy source header\n1249\t\tcopySource := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\")\n1250\t\tif copySource == \"\" {\n1251\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing x-amz-copy-source header\", 400)\n1252\t\t\treturn\n1253\t\t}\n1254\t\n1255\t\t// Parse source bucket and key\n1256\t\t// Format: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n1257\t\tsrcBucket, srcKey := parseCopySource(copySource)\n1258\t\tif srcBucket == \"\" || srcKey == \"\" {\n1259\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidCopySource\", \"Invalid copy source format\", 400)\n1260\t\t\treturn\n1261\t\t}\n1262\t\n1263\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n1264\t\tsrcPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(srcKey)\n1265\t\tdstPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(dstKey)\n1266\t\n1267\t\t// Get source object metadata\n1268\t\tsrcInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey)\n1269\t\tif err != nil {\n1270\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Source object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1271\t\t\treturn\n1272\t\t}\n1273\t\n1274\t\t// Check metadata directive\n1275\t\tmetadataDirective := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-metadata-directive\")\n1276\t\treplaceMetadata := metadataDirective == \"REPLACE\"\n1277\t\n1278\t\t// Build response XML structure\n1279\t\ttype CopyObjectResult struct {\n1280\t\t\tXMLName      xml.Name `xml:\"CopyObjectResult\"`\n1281\t\t\tLastModified string   `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n1282\t\t\tETag         string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n1283\t\t}\n1284\t\n1285\t\t// Handle ARMOR-encrypted objects\n1286\t\tif srcInfo.IsARMOREncrypted {\n1287\t\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n1288\t\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(srcInfo.Metadata)\n1289\t\t\tif !ok {\n1290\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n1291\t\t\t\treturn\n1292\t\t\t}\n1293\t\n1294\t\t\t// Get the source MEK using the key ID from metadata\n1295\t\t\tsrcMEK, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n1296\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1297\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get source decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1298\t\t\t\treturn\n1299\t\t\t}\n1300\t\n1301\t\t\t// Unwrap DEK with source MEK\n1302\t\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(srcMEK, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n1303\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1304\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1305\t\t\t\treturn\n1306\t\t\t}\n1307\t\n1308\t\t\t// Get the destination MEK for the target key\n1309\t\t\tdstMEK, dstKeyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(dstKey)\n1310\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1311\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get destination encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1312\t\t\t\treturn\n1313\t\t\t}\n1314\t\n1315\t\t\t// Re-wrap DEK with destination MEK (handles key rotation and cross-prefix copy)\n1316\t\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(dstMEK, dek)\n1317\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1318\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to re-wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1319\t\t\t\treturn\n1320\t\t\t}\n1321\t\n1322\t\t\t// Build new metadata with re-wrapped DEK and destination key ID\n1323\t\t\tnewMeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n1324\t\t\t\tVersion:       armorMeta.Version,\n1325\t\t\t\tBlockSize:     armorMeta.BlockSize,\n1326\t\t\t\tPlaintextSize: armorMeta.PlaintextSize,\n1327\t\t\t\tContentType:   armorMeta.ContentType,\n1328\t\t\t\tIV:            armorMeta.IV,\n1329\t\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n1330\t\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  armorMeta.PlaintextSHA,\n1331\t\t\t\tETag:          armorMeta.ETag,\n1332\t\t\t\tKeyID:         dstKeyID,\n1333\t\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n1334\t\n1335\t\t\t// Handle REPLACE directive - copy custom metadata headers from request\n1336\t\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n1337\t\t\t\t// Check for new content-type in request\n1338\t\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n1339\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-content-type\"] = ct\n1340\t\t\t\t}\n1341\t\t\t\t// Copy any additional custom headers from request\n1342\t\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n1343\t\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") && !strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-Armor-\") {\n1344\t\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n1345\t\t\t\t\t}\n1346\t\t\t\t}\n1347\t\t\t}\n1348\t\n1349\t\t\t// Perform server-side copy with updated metadata\n1350\t\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, newMeta, true); err != nil {\n1351\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n1352\t\t\t\treturn\n1353\t\t\t}\n1354\t\n1355\t\t\t// Record provenance for the copy\n1356\t\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(dstKey) {\n1357\t\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, dstKey, armorMeta.PlaintextSHA, \"copy\")\n1358\t\t\t}\n1359\t\n1360\t\t\t// Invalidate cache for destination\n1361\t\t\th.cache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n1362\t\t\th.footerCache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n1363\t\n1364\t\t\t// Return success response\n1365\t\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n1366\t\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1367\t\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag),\n1368\t\t\t}\n1369\t\n1370\t\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1371\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1372\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1373\t\t\t\treturn\n1374\t\t\t}\n1375\t\n1376\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1377\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1378\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1379\t\t\tw.Write(output)\n1380\t\t\treturn\n1381\t\t}\n1382\t\n1383\t\t// Non-ARMOR object - pass through copy\n1384\t\tvar meta map[string]string\n1385\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n1386\t\t\tmeta = make(map[string]string)\n1387\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n1388\t\t\t\tmeta[\"Content-Type\"] = ct\n1389\t\t\t}\n1390\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n1391\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") {\n1392\t\t\t\t\tmeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n1393\t\t\t\t}\n1394\t\t\t}\n1395\t\t}\n1396\t\n1397\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, meta, replaceMetadata); err != nil {\n1398\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n1399\t\t\treturn\n1400\t\t}\n1401\t\n1402\t\t// Get the destination object info for ETag\n1403\t\tdstInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey)\n1404\t\tif err != nil {\n1405\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to get destination info\", 500)\n1406\t\t\treturn\n1407\t\t}\n1408\t\n1409\t\t// Return success response\n1410\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n1411\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1412\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, dstInfo.ETag),\n1413\t\t}\n1414\t\n1415\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1416\t\tif err != nil {\n1417\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1418\t\t\treturn\n1419\t\t}\n1420\t\n1421\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1422\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1423\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1424\t\tw.Write(output)\n1425\t}\n1426\t\n1427\t// parseCopySource parses the x-amz-copy-source header value.\n1428\t// Supports formats: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n1429\tfunc parseCopySource(copySource string) (bucket, key string) {\n1430\t\t// Remove leading slash if present\n1431\t\tcopySource = strings.TrimPrefix(copySource, \"/\")\n1432\t\n1433\t\t// URL decode the key portion (keys may contain special characters)\n1434\t\tif idx := strings.Index(copySource, \"/\"); idx != -1 {\n1435\t\t\tbucket = copySource[:idx]\n1436\t\t\tkey = copySource[idx+1:]\n1437\t\t\t// URL decode the key\n1438\t\t\tif decoded, err := url.QueryUnescape(key); err == nil {\n1439\t\t\t\tkey = decoded\n1440\t\t\t}\n1441\t\t}\n1442\t\n1443\t\treturn bucket, key\n1444\t}\n1445\t\n1446\t// enrichPlaintextSizes replaces encrypted on-disk sizes with plaintext sizes for\n1447\t// ARMOR-encrypted objects. B2's ListObjectsV2 response includes the encrypted\n1448\t// object size; we resolve the plaintext size using the metadata cache (if warm)\n1449\t// or a parallel HeadObject call per object (on cache miss). Each errgroup goroutine\n1450\t// writes to a distinct slice index so no mutex is needed.\n1451\tfunc (h *Handlers) enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx context.Context, bucket string, result *backend.ListResult) {\n1452\t\teg, egCtx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n1453\t\tfor i := range result.Objects {\n1454\t\t\ti := i\n1455\t\t\tkey := result.Objects[i].Key\n1456\t\t\tif am, ok := h.cache.Get(bucket, key); ok {\n1457\t\t\t\tif am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n1458\t\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = am.PlaintextSize\n1459\t\t\t\t}\n1460\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1461\t\t\t}\n1462\t\t\teg.Go(func() error {\n1463\t\t\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(egCtx, bucket, key)\n1464\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1465\t\t\t\t\treturn nil // non-fatal: keep encrypted size rather than failing the listing\n1466\t\t\t\t}\n1467\t\t\t\tif info.Size > 0 {\n1468\t\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = info.Size\n1469\t\t\t\t}\n1470\t\t\t\treturn nil\n1471\t\t\t})\n1472\t\t}\n1473\t\teg.Wait() //nolint:errcheck\n1474\t}\n1475\t\n1476\t// ListObjectsV2 handles S3 ListObjectsV2.\n1477\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListObjectsV2(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1478\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1479\t\n1480\t\tprefix := r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n1481\t\tdelimiter := r.URL.Query().Get(\"delimiter\")\n1482\t\tcontToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"continuation-token\")\n1483\t\tmaxKeys := 1000\n1484\t\tif mk := r.URL.Query().Get(\"max-keys\"); mk != \"\" {\n1485\t\t\tif v, err := strconv.Atoi(mk); err == nil && v > 0 {\n1486\t\t\t\tmaxKeys = v\n1487\t\t\t}\n1488\t\t}\n1489\t\n1490\t\t// Apply the configured prefix to the prefix parameter for backend operations.\n1491\t\t// When ARMOR_PREFIX is set, the backend stores all keys with the prefix prepended,\n1492\t\t// but clients don't know about it. We need to prepend the prefix to the client's\n1493\t\t// requested prefix so the backend finds the right objects.\n1494\t\tbackendPrefix := h.applyPrefix(prefix)\n1495\t\n1496\t\tvar result *backend.ListResult\n1497\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1498\t\t\tif cached, ok := h.listCache.Get(bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken); ok {\n1499\t\t\t\tresult = cached\n1500\t\t\t}\n1501\t\t}\n1502\t\tif result == nil {\n1503\t\t\tvar err error\n1504\t\t\tresult, err = h.backend.List(ctx, bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, contToken, maxKeys)\n1505\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1506\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list: %v\", err), 500)\n1507\t\t\t\treturn\n1508\t\t\t}\n1509\t\t\t// Resolve plaintext sizes for ARMOR-encrypted objects before caching.\n1510\t\t\t// B2 ListObjectsV2 returns encrypted (on-disk) sizes; clients like DuckDB\n1511\t\t\t// use these sizes to compute byte-range offsets, so they must reflect the\n1512\t\t\t// plaintext length.\n1513\t\t\th.enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx, bucket, result)\n1514\t\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1515\t\t\t\th.listCache.Set(bucket, prefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken, result)\n1516\t\t\t}\n1517\t\t}\n1518\t\n1519\t\t// Build XML response\n1520\t\ttype Contents struct {\n1521\t\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1522\t\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n1523\t\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n1524\t\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n1525\t\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n1526\t\t}\n1527\t\n1528\t\t// S3 spec: each CommonPrefixes entry is a separate wrapper element with one\n1529\t\t// Prefix child. Using []string with \"Outer>Inner\" produces an empty\n1530\t\t// <CommonPrefixes/> when the slice is nil, which the AWS SDK v1 parses as a\n1531\t\t// slice with one nil element — causing nil-pointer crashes in clients like\n1532\t\t// litestream. Using a dedicated struct + omitempty avoids this.\n1533\t\ttype CommonPrefix struct {\n1534\t\t\tPrefix string `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n1535\t\t}\n1536\t\n1537\t\ttype ListBucketResult struct {\n1538\t\t\tXMLName               xml.Name       `xml:\"ListBucketResult\"`\n1539\t\t\tXmlns                 string         `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1540\t\t\tName                  string         `xml:\"Name\"`\n1541\t\t\tPrefix                string         `xml:\"Prefix\"`\n1542\t\t\tDelimiter             string         `xml:\"Delimiter,omitempty\"`\n1543\t\t\tMaxKeys               int            `xml:\"MaxKeys\"`\n1544\t\t\tIsTruncated           bool           `xml:\"IsTruncated\"`\n1545\t\t\tNextContinuationToken string         `xml:\"NextContinuationToken,omitempty\"`\n1546\t\t\tContents              []Contents\n1547\t\t\tCommonPrefixes        []CommonPrefix `xml:\"CommonPrefixes,omitempty\"`\n1548\t\t}\n1549\t\n1550\t\tresp := ListBucketResult{\n1551\t\t\tXmlns:                 \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1552\t\t\tName:                  bucket,\n1553\t\t\tPrefix:                prefix,\n1554\t\t\tDelimiter:             delimiter,\n1555\t\t\tMaxKeys:               maxKeys,\n1556\t\t\tIsTruncated:           result.IsTruncated,\n1557\t\t\tNextContinuationToken: result.NextToken,\n1558\t\t}\n1559\t\n1560\t\tfor _, obj := range result.Objects {\n1561\t\t\t// Strip the configured prefix from object keys before returning to client.\n1562\t\t\t// Clients don't know about the prefix, so we need to remove it from the keys.\n1563\t\t\tstrippedKey := h.stripPrefix(obj.Key)\n1564\t\t\tresp.Contents = append(resp.Contents, Contents{\n1565\t\t\t\tKey:          strippedKey,\n1566\t\t\t\tLastModified: obj.LastModified.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1567\t\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, obj.ETag),\n1568\t\t\t\tSize:         obj.Size,\n1569\t\t\t\tStorageClass: \"STANDARD\",\n1570\t\t\t})\n1571\t\t}\n1572\t\n1573\t\tfor _, p := range result.CommonPrefixes {\n1574\t\t\t// Strip the configured prefix from common prefixes before returning to client.\n1575\t\t\t// Common prefixes are used for directory-like listings with delimiters.\n1576\t\t\tstrippedPrefix := h.stripPrefixFromCommonPrefix(p)\n1577\t\t\tresp.CommonPrefixes = append(resp.CommonPrefixes, CommonPrefix{Prefix: strippedPrefix})\n1578\t\t}\n1579\t\n1580\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n1581\t\tif err != nil {\n1582\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1583\t\t\treturn\n1584\t\t}\n1585\t\n1586\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1587\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1588\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1589\t\tw.Write(output)\n1590\t}\n1591\t\n1592\t// Stub implementations for other operations\n1593\t\n1594\t// HeadBucket handles S3 HeadBucket.\n1595\tfunc (h *Handlers) HeadBucket(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1596\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1597\t\n1598\t\tif err := h.backend.HeadBucket(ctx, bucket); err != nil {\n1599\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchBucket\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Bucket not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1600\t\t\treturn\n1601\t\t}\n1602\t\n1603\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1604\t}\n1605\t\n1606\t// GetBucketLocation handles S3 GetBucketLocation (GET ?location).\n1607\t// Returns a static LocationConstraint so MinIO/Forgejo clients can determine the region\n1608\t// without requiring region-aware routing.\n1609\tfunc (h contains XML with a list of objects to delete.\n1690\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObjects(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1691\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1692\t\n1693\t\t// Parse \"application/xml\")\n1781\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1782\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1783\t\tw.Write(output)\n1784\t}\n1785\t\n1786\t// ListBuckets CreateMultipartUpload handles S3 CreateMultipartUpload with ARMOR encryption.\n1840\t// It generates a DEK and IV, wraps the DEK, and stores the state in B2.\n1841\tfunc (h *Handlers) CreateMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1842\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1843\t\n1844\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n1845\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n1846\t\tif err != nil {\n1847\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1848\t\t\treturn\n1849\t\t}\n1850\t\n1851\t\t// Generate DEK and IV for this upload\n1852\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n1853\t\tif err != nil {\n1854\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1855\t\t\treturn\n1856\t\t}\n1857\t\n1858\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n1859\t\tif err != nil {\n1860\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n1861\t\t\treturn\n1862\t\t}\n1863\t\n1864\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n1865\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n1866\t\tif err != nil {\n1867\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1868\t\t\treturn\n1869\t\t}\n1870\t\n1871\t\t// Get content type\n1872\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n1873\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n1874\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n1875\t\t}\n1876\t\n1877\t\t// Initiate multipart upload with B2 (no ARMOR metadata yet - that comes on completion)\n1878\t\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, nil)\n1879\t\tif err != nil {\n1880\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n1881\t\t\treturn\n1882\t\t}\n1883\t\n1884\t\t// Save multipart state to B2\n1885\t\tstate := &backend.MultipartState{\n1886\t\t\tUploadID:       uploadID,\n1887\t\t\tBucket:         bucket,\n1888\t\t\tKey:            key,\n1889\t\t\tIV:             iv,\n1890\t\t\tWrappedDEK:     wrappedDEK,\n1891\t\t\tBlockSize:      h.config.BlockSize,\n1892\t\t\tCreated:        time.Now(),\n1893\t\t\tContentType:    contentType,\n1894\t\t\tKeyID:          keyID,\n1895\t\t\tEncryptedBytes: 0,\n1896\t\t\tPartHMACs:      make(map[int]string),\n1897\t\t\tPartSizes:      make(map[int]int64),\n1898\t\t}\n1899\t\n1900\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1901\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n1902\t\t\t// Try to abort the upload on state save failure\n1903\t\t\th.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n1904\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n1905\t\t\treturn\n1906\t\t}\n1907\t\n1908\t\t// Build XML response\n1909\t\ttype InitiateMultipartUploadResult struct {\n1910\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"InitiateMultipartUploadResult\"`\n1911\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1912\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n1913\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n1914\t\t\tUploadID string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n1915\t\t}\n1916\t\n1917\t\tresult := InitiateMultipartUploadResult{\n1918\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1919\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n1920\t\t\tKey:      key,\n1921\t\t\tUploadID: uploadID,\n1922\t\t}\n1923\t\n1924\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1925\t\tif err != nil {\n1926\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1927\t\t\treturn\n1928\t\t}\n1929\t\n1930\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1931\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1932\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1933\t\tw.Write(output)\n1934\t}\n1935\t\n1936\t// UploadPart handles S3 UploadPart with encryption.\n1937\t// Each part is encrypted with a CTR counter offset based on cumulative encrypted bytes.\n1938\tfunc (h *Handlers) UploadPart(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n1939\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1940\t\n1941\t\t// Parse part number\n1942\t\tpartNumberStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\")\n1943\t\tif partNumberStr == \"\" {\n1944\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing partNumber\", 400)\n1945\t\t\treturn\n1946\t\t}\n1947\t\tpartNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(partNumberStr, 10, 32)\n1948\t\tif err != nil || partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n1949\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Invalid partNumber\", 400)\n1950\t\t\treturn\n1951\t\t}\n1952\t\n1953\t\t// Load multipart state\n1954\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1955\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n1956\t\tif err != nil {\n1957\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1958\t\t\treturn\n1959\t\t}\n1960\t\n1961\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n1962\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n1963\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n1964\t\t\treturn\n1965\t\t}\n1966\t\n1967\t\t// Read plaintext part\n1968\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n1969\t\tif err != nil {\n1970\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n1971\t\t\treturn\n1972\t\t}\n1973\t\n1974\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n1975\t\n1976\t\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n1977\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(state.KeyID)\n1978\t\tif err != nil {\n1979\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1980\t\t\treturn\n1981\t\t}\n1982\t\n1983\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n1984\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, state.WrappedDEK)\n1985\t\tif err != nil {\n1986\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1987\t\t\treturn\n1988\t\t}\n1989\t\n1990\t\t// Calculate starting block index for CTR counter\n1991\t\t// Each block is blockSize bytes, so counter = encryptedBytes / blockSize\n1992\t\tstartBlockIndex := uint32(state.EncryptedBytes / int64(state.BlockSize))\n1993\t\n1994\t\t// Create encryptor with the part's starting counter\n1995\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptorWithCounter(dek, state.IV, state.BlockSize, startBlockIndex)\n1996\t\tif err != nil {\n1997\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n1998\t\t\treturn\n1999\t\t}\n2000\t\n2001\t\t// Encrypt the part\n2002\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n2003\t\tif err != nil {\n2004\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n2005\t\t\treturn\n2006\t\t}\n2007\t\n2008\t\t// Derive HMAC key\n2009\t\thmacKey := crypto.DeriveHMACKey(dek)\n2010\t\n2011\t\t// Compute block HMACs\n2012\t\tblockHMACs, err := backend.ComputeBlockHMACs(encrypted, state.BlockSize, hmacKey, startBlockIndex)\n2013\t\tif err != nil {\n2014\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to compute HMACs: %v\", err), 500)\n2015\t\t\treturn\n2016\t\t}\n2017\t\n2018\t\t// Upload encrypted part to B2\n2019\t\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, int32(partNumber), bytes.NewReader(encrypted), int64(len(encrypted)))\n2020\t\tif err != nil {\n2021\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n2022\t\t\treturn\n2023\t\t}\n2024\t\n2025\t\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n2026\t\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n2027\t\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n2028\t\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n2029\t\n2030\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n2031\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n2032\t\t\treturn\n2033\t\t}\n2034\t\n2035\t\t// Return ETag - suppress unused variable warning\n2036\t\t_ = hmacTable\n2037\t\n2038\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n2039\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2040\t}\n2041\t\n2042\t// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n2043\t// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\n2044\tfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2045\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2046\t\n2047\t\t// Load multipart state\n2048\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2049\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2050\t\tif err != nil {\n2051\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2052\t\t\treturn\n2053\t\t}\n2054\t\n2055\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2056\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2057\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2058\t\t\treturn\n2059\t\t}\n2060\t\n2061\t\t// Parse the CompleteMultipartUpload request XML\n2062\t\ttype Part struct {\n2063\t\t\tPartNumber int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n2064\t\t\tETag       string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2065\t\t}\n2066\t\n2067\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadReq struct {\n2068\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n2069\t\t\tParts   []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n2070\t\t}\n2071\t\n2072\t\tvar completeReq CompleteMultipartUploadReq\n2073\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n2074\t\tif err != nil {\n2075\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n2076\t\t\treturn\n2077\t\t}\n2078\t\n2079\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &completeReq); err != nil {\n2080\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n2081\t\t\treturn\n2082\t\t}\n2083\t\n2084\t\tif len(completeReq.Parts) == 0 {\n2085\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"No parts specified\", 400)\n2086\t\t\treturn\n2087\t\t}\n2088\t\n2089\t\t// Convert to backend.CompletedPart\n2090\t\tparts := make([]backend.CompletedPart, len(completeReq.Parts))\n2091\t\tfor i, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2092\t\t\tparts[i] = backend.CompletedPart{\n2093\t\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(p.PartNumber),\n2094\t\t\t\tETag:       p.ETag,\n2095\t\t\t}\n2096\t\t}\n2097\t\n2098\t\t// Calculate total plaintext size\n2099\t\tvar totalPlaintextSize int64\n2100\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2101\t\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2102\t\t\t\ttotalPlaintextSize += size\n2103\t\t\t}\n2104\t\t}\n2105\t\n2106\t\t// Assemble all block HMACs in order\n2107\t\tvar allBlockHMACs [][]byte\n2108\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2109\t\t\tif hmacsBase64, ok := state.PartHMACs[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2110\t\t\t\thmacs, err := backend.DecodeHMACFromBase64(hmacsBase64)\n2111\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2112\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode HMACs for part %d: %v\", p.PartNumber, err), 500)\n2113\t\t\t\t\treturn\n2114\t\t\t\t}\n2115\t\t\t\tallBlockHMACs = append(allBlockHMACs, hmacs...)\n2116\t\t\t}\n2117\t\t}\n2118\t\n2119\t\t// Complete the multipart upload in B2\n2120\t\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n2121\t\tif err != nil {\n2122\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to complete multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n2123\t\t\treturn\n2124\t\t}\n2125\t\n2126\t\t// Store HMAC table as sidecar\n2127\t\tif err := manager.SaveHMACTable(ctx, key, allBlockHMACs, state.BlockSize); err != nil {\n2128\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n2129\t\t\treturn\n2130\t\t}\n2131\t\n2132\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256 (we don't have the full plaintext, so use a placeholder)\n2133\t\t// In a full implementation, we'd track SHA during upload\n2134\t\tplaintextSHA := sha256.Sum256([]byte{})\n2135\t\n2136\t\t// Build ARMOR metadata and update via CopyObject\n2137\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n2138\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n2139\t\t\tBlockSize:     state.BlockSize,\n2140\t\t\tPlaintextSize: totalPlaintextSize,\n2141\t\t\tContentType:   state.ContentType,\n2142\t\t\tIV:            state.IV,\n2143\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    state.WrappedDEK,\n2144\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n2145\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n2146\t\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n2147\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n2148\t\n2149\t\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n2150\t\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n2151\t\n2152\t\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n2153\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n2154\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n2155\t\t\treturn\n2156\t\t}\n2157\t\n2158\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2159\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2160\t\n2161\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n2162\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n2163\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, totalPlaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), state.IV, state.WrappedDEK, state.BlockSize, state.ContentType, etag)\n2164\t\t}\n2165\t\n2166\t\t// Record provenance for the multipart upload\n2167\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n2168\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), \"multipart\")\n2169\t\t}\n2170\t\n2171\t\t// Build XML response\n2172\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadResult struct {\n2173\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUploadResult\"`\n2174\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2175\t\t\tLocation string   `xml:\"Location\"`\n2176\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n2177\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n2178\t\t\tETag     string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2179\t\t}\n2180\t\n2181\t\tresult := CompleteMultipartUploadResult{\n2182\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2183\t\t\tLocation: fmt.Sprintf(\"/%s/%s\", bucket, key),\n2184\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n2185\t\t\tKey:      key,\n2186\t\t\tETag: 500)\n2192\t\t\treturn\n2193\t\t}\n2194\t\n2195\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n2196\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2197\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n2198\t\tw.Write(output)\n2199\t}\n2200\t\n2201\t// AbortMultipartUpload handles S3 AbortMultipartUpload.\n2202\t// It deletes the multipart state and forwards the abort to B2.\n2203\tfunc (h *Handlers) AbortMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2204\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2205\t\n2206\t\t// Load multipart state to \"application/xml\")\n2705\t\tw.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n2706\t\tvar codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n2707\t\txml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n2708\t\txml.EscapeText(&msgBuf,
