Package server implements the ARMOR S3-compatible HTTP server.\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/cipher\"\n\t\"crypto/hmac\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/binary\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"golang.org/x/sync/errgroup\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/backend\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/b2keys\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/canary\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/crypto\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/dashboard\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/keymanager\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/logging\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/manifest\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/metrics\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/presign\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/provenance\"\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/server/handlers\"\n)\n\n// Server represents the ARMOR server.\ntype Server struct {\n\tconfig         *config.Config\n\tbackend        backend.Backend\n\tcache          *backend.MetadataCache\n\tfooterCache    *backend.FooterCache\n\tlistCache      *backend.ListCache\n\tkeyManager     *keymanager.KeyManager\n\tcanary         *canary.Monitor\n\tprovenance     *provenance.Manager\n\tpresigner      *presign.Signer\n\tb2keys         *b2keys.Client // B2 native API key management\n\tdashboard      *dashboard.Dashboard\n\tmanifest           *manifest.Index      // in-memory metadata index (nil when disabled)\n\tmanifestWriter     *manifest.Writer     // async delta writer (nil when disabled)\n\tmanifestCompactor  *manifest.Compactor  // background compaction goroutine (nil when disabled)\n\n\t// canaryStarted tracks whether the canary monitor has been started\n\tcanaryStarted bool\n\n\t// canaryDisabled skips the canary check in /readyz when true\n\tcanaryDisabled bool\n\n\t// Metrics and request tracking\n\tmetrics       *metrics.Metrics\n\trequestTracker *metrics.RequestTracker\n\tlogger        *logging.Logger\n}\n\n// New creates a new ARMOR server.\nfunc New(cfg *config.Config) (*Server, error) {\n\t// Create B2 backend\n\tb2Backend, err := backend.NewB2Backend(context.Background(), backend.B2Config{\n\t\tRegion:      cfg.B2Region,\n\t\tEndpoint:    cfg.B2Endpoint,\n\t\tAccessKeyID: cfg.B2AccessKeyID,\n\t\tSecretKey:   cfg.B2SecretAccessKey,\n\t\tCFDomain:    cfg.CFDomain,\n\t})\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create B2 backend: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create metadata cache\n\tcache := backend.NewMetadataCache(cfg.CacheMaxEntries, cfg.CacheTTL)\n\n\t// Create footer cache (for Parquet footer pinning)\n\tfooterCache := backend.NewFooterCache(cfg.CacheMaxEntries, cfg.CacheTTL)\n\n\t// Create list cache (nil when TTL is 0, meaning disabled)\n\tvar listCache *backend.ListCache\n\tif cfg.ListCacheTTL > 0 {\n\t\tlistCache = backend.NewListCache(cfg.ListCacheMaxEntries, cfg.ListCacheTTL)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create key manager\n\t// Convert config.KeyRoutes to keymanager.Route format\n\tvar routes []keymanager.Route\n\tfor _, r := range cfg.KeyRoutes {\n\t\troutes = append(routes, keymanager.Route{\n\t\t\tPrefix:  r.Prefix,\n\t\t\tKeyName: r.KeyName,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\tkeyMgr, err := keymanager.New(cfg.MEK, cfg.NamedKeys, routes)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create key manager: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Create canary monitor\n\tcanaryMonitor := canary.NewMonitor(canary.Config{\n\t\tBackend:    b2Backend,\n\t\tBucket:     cfg.Bucket,\n\t\tMEK:        cfg.MEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:  cfg.BlockSize,\n\t\tInstanceID: cfg.WriterID,\n\t\tInterval:   5 * time.Minute,\n\t\tCanarySize: 1024,\n\t\tMaxRetries: 3,\n\t\tRetryDelay: 10 * time.Second,\n\t})\n\n\t// Create provenance manager\n\tprovenanceMgr := provenance.NewManager(b2Backend, cfg.Bucket, cfg.WriterID)\n\n\t// Create logger\n\tlogger := logging.New(\"armor\")\n\n\t// Create presign signer\n\tpresigner := presign.NewSigner(cfg.PresignSecret, cfg.PresignBaseURL)\n\n\t// Create B2 keys client (for B2 native API key management)\n\t// Uses the same B2 credentials as the S3 backend\n\tvar b2keysClient *b2keys.Client\n\tif cfg.Bucket != \"\" {\n\t\t// Bucket-scoped configuration: application key is restricted to a single bucket\n\t\t// and cannot call account-level B2 API endpoints (like b2_authorize_account).\n\t\t// Key management is unavailable in this mode, which is expected and intentional.\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"B2 key management disabled (application key is bucket-scoped)\")\n\t} else {\n\t\t// No bucket restriction: attempt to create B2 keys client for account-level operations\n\t\t// Extract account ID from the key ID (B2 key IDs are in format accountIdKeyId)\n\t\t// The account ID is the first 12 characters\n\t\taccountID := cfg.B2AccessKeyID\n\t\tif len(accountID) > 12 {\n\t\t\taccountID = accountID[:12]\n\t\t}\n\t\tb2keysClient, err = b2keys.NewClient(context.Background(), accountID, cfg.B2SecretAccessKey)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Warn(\"Failed to create B2 keys client - key management disabled\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create dashboard with optional authentication\n\tdash := dashboard.NewWithAuth(b2Backend, cfg.Bucket, metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n\t\tcfg.DashboardUser, cfg.DashboardPass, cfg.DashboardToken)\n\n\t// Load manifest index from B2 (startup load).\n\t// The manifest is a performance optimisation — errors are logged as warnings\n\t// and do not prevent startup.\n\tvar manifestIdx *manifest.Index\n\tif cfg.ManifestEnabled {\n\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New()\n\t\t// ListRaw bypasses the .armor/ filter so manifest keys are discoverable.\n\t\tlister := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Fetch snapshot and delta blobs via Cloudflare for free egress and edge\n\t\t// caching. Fall back to direct B2 if no Cloudflare domain is configured.\n\t\tfetcher := func(ctx context.Context, key string) ([]byte, error) {\n\t\t\tvar body io.ReadCloser\n\t\t\tvar ferr error\n\t\t\tif cfg.CFDomain != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.Get(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tbody, _, ferr = b2Backend.GetDirect(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif ferr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, ferr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\t\t\treturn io.ReadAll(body)\n\t\t}\n\t\tloadStart := time.Now()\n\t\tif loadErr := manifest.Load(context.Background(), manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, lister, fetcher); loadErr != nil {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": loadErr.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Warn(\"manifest startup load failed — continuing with empty manifest index\")\n\t\t\tmanifestIdx = manifest.New() // reset to empty on error\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tlogger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"entries\":  manifestIdx.Len(),\n\t\t\t\t\"seq\":      manifestIdx.Seq(),\n\t\t\t\t\"duration\": time.Since(loadStart).String(),\n\t\t\t}).Info(\"manifest index loaded\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Create and start the manifest writer (async delta persistence to B2).\n\t// Uploads go direct to B2 (free ingress); no Cloudflare path needed here.\n\tvar manifestWriter *manifest.Writer\n\tvar manifestCompactor *manifest.Compactor\n\tif manifestIdx != nil {\n\t\tuploader := func(ctx context.Context, key string, data []byte) error {\n\t\t\treturn b2Backend.Put(ctx, cfg.Bucket, key, bytes.NewReader(data), int64(len(data)), nil)\n\t\t}\n\t\tmanifestWriter = manifest.NewWriter(manifestIdx, cfg.ManifestPrefix, cfg.WriterID, uploader, 0)\n\n\t\t// Create compactor that lists and batch-deletes via the B2 backend.\n\t\tlisterForCompactor := func(ctx context.Context, prefix, token string) ([]string, string, error) {\n\t\t\tresult, lerr := b2Backend.ListRaw(ctx, cfg.Bucket, prefix, \"\", token, 1000)\n\t\t\tif lerr != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, \"\", lerr\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(result.Objects))\n\t\t\tfor i, obj := range result.Objects {\n\t\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn keys, result.NextToken, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tdeleter := func(ctx context.Context, keys []string) error {\n\t\t\treturn b2Backend.DeleteObjects(ctx, cfg.Bucket, keys)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcompactionInterval := time.Duration(cfg.ManifestCompactionInterval) * time.Second\n\t\tmanifestCompactor = manifest.NewCompactor(\n\t\t\tmanifestIdx,\n\t\t\tcfg.ManifestPrefix,\n\t\t\tcfg.WriterID,\n\t\t\tuploader,\n\t\t\tlisterForCompactor,\n\t\t\tdeleter,\n\t\t\tcompactionInterval,\n\t\t\tcfg.ManifestCompactionThreshold,\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// Wire writer → compactor: after each delta flush, notify compactor.\n\t\tmanifestWriter.SetOnFlush(manifestCompactor.NotifyDelta)\n\n\t\tmanifestWriter.Start(context.Background())\n\t\tmanifestCompactor.Start(context.Background())\n\t\tlogger.Info(\"manifest writer and compactor started\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn &Server{\n\t\tconfig:         cfg,\n\t\tbackend:        b2Backend,\n\t\tcache:          cache,\n\t\tfooterCache:    footerCache,\n\t\tlistCache:      listCache,\n\t\tkeyManager:     keyMgr,\n\t\tcanary:         canaryMonitor,\n\t\tcanaryDisabled: cfg.CanaryDisabled,\n\t\tprovenance:     provenanceMgr,\n\t\tpresigner:      presigner,\n\t\tb2keys:         b2keysClient,\n\t\tdashboard:      dash,\n\t\tmanifest:          manifestIdx,\n\t\tmanifestWriter:    manifestWriter,\n\t\tmanifestCompactor: manifestCompactor,\n\t\tmetrics:        metrics.DefaultMetrics,\n\t\trequestTracker: metrics.DefaultRequestTracker,\n\t\tlogger:         logger,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// manifestRecorder implements handlers.ManifestRecorder. It updates the\n// in-memory index synchronously (so subsequent HeadObject calls see the new\n// state immediately) and enqueues the op for async B2 delta persistence.\ntype manifestRecorder struct {\n\tidx    *manifest.Index\n\twriter *manifest.Writer\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string) {\n\tentry := &manifest.Entry{\n\t\tPlaintextSize:   size,\n\t\tPlaintextSHA256: sha256Hex,\n\t\tIV:              iv,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:      wrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:       blockSize,\n\t\tContentType:     contentType,\n\t\tETag:            etag,\n\t\tLastModified:    time.Now().UTC(),\n\t}\n\tm.idx.Put(bucket, key, entry)\n\tm.writer.EnqueuePut(bucket, key, entry)\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) RecordDelete(bucket, key string) {\n\tm.idx.Delete(bucket, key)\n\tm.writer.EnqueueDelete(bucket, key)\n}\n\nfunc (m *manifestRecorder) Lookup(bucket, key string) (*handlers.ManifestEntry, bool) {\n\tentry, ok := m.idx.Get(bucket, key)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn nil, false\n\t}\n\treturn &handlers.ManifestEntry{\n\t\tPlaintextSize: entry.PlaintextSize,\n\t\tContentType:   entry.ContentType,\n\t\tETag:          entry.ETag,\n\t\tLastModified:  entry.LastModified,\n\t\tIV:            entry.IV,\n\t\tWrappedDEK:    entry.WrappedDEK,\n\t\tBlockSize:     entry.BlockSize,\n\t}, true\n}\n\n// StopManifestWriter flushes any pending manifest ops and stops the async writer.\nfunc (s *Server) StopManifestWriter() {\n\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\ts.manifestWriter.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest writer stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// StopManifestCompactor stops the background compaction goroutine.\nfunc (s *Server) StopManifestCompactor() {\n\tif s.manifestCompactor != nil {\n\t\ts.manifestCompactor.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"manifest compactor stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// StartCanary starts the canary monitor.\n// It should be called after the server is created.\n// When ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true, the monitor is not started and /readyz\n// always returns 200 regardless of B2 reachability.\nfunc (s *Server) StartCanary(ctx context.Context) {\n\tif s.canary == nil || s.canaryStarted {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true)\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.canaryStarted = true\n\ts.canary.Start(ctx)\n\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor started\")\n}\n\n// StopCanary stops the canary monitor.\nfunc (s *Server) StopCanary() {\n\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n\t\ts.canary.Stop()\n\t\ts.logger.Info(\"Canary monitor stopped\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// Handler returns the main S3 API handler.\nfunc (s *Server) Handler() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\t// Health endpoints\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/readyz\", s.readyz)\n\n\t// Share endpoint for pre-signed URLs (public, no auth required)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/share/\", s.handleShare)\n\n\t// S3 operations\n\th := handlers.New(s.config, s.backend, s.cache, s.footerCache, s.keyManager, s.listCache)\n\n\t// Wire up provenance if available\n\tif s.provenance != nil {\n\t\th.WithProvenance(s.provenance)\n\t}\n\n\t// Wire up manifest recorder if enabled\n\tif s.manifest != nil && s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\th.WithManifest(&manifestRecorder{idx: s.manifest, writer: s.manifestWriter})\n\t}\n\n\t// Bucket operations\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/\", s.wrapHandler(h.HandleRoot))\n\n\treturn mux\n}\n\n// AdminHandler returns the admin API handler.\nfunc (s *Server) AdminHandler() http.Handler {\n\tmux := http.NewServeMux()\n\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/healthz\", s.healthz)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/verify\", s.verifyKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/rotate\", s.rotateKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/key/export\", s.exportKey)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/canary\", s.canaryHandler)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/armor/audit\", s.audit)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/presign\", s.handlePresign)\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys\", s.handleB2Keys)           // GET=List, POST=Create\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/admin/b2/keys/\", s.handleB2KeyDelete)     // DELETE=Delete by ID\n\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/metrics\", s.metrics.Handler())\n\n\t// Dashboard routes (authenticated via DashboardUser/Pass or DashboardToken)\n\tif s.dashboard != nil {\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/\", s.dashboard.HandlerWithAuth()) // For prefix navigation\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/object\", s.dashboard.ObjectDetailHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/metrics\", s.dashboard.MetricsHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/encryption-stats\", s.dashboard.EncryptionStatsHandlerWithAuth())\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/api/list\", s.dashboard.ListAPIHandlerWithAuth())\n\n\t\t// Key rotation proxy handler (authenticated)\n\t\tadminClient := &http.Client{\n\t\t\tTimeout: 30 * time.Minute, // Key rotation can take a long time\n\t\t}\n\t\tadminURL := \"http://\" + s.config.AdminListen + \"/admin/key/rotate\"\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/rotate\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateHandlerWithAuth(adminClient, adminURL))\n\t\tmux.HandleFunc(\"/dashboard/admin/key/status\", s.dashboard.KeyRotateStatusHandlerWithAuth())\n\t}\n\n\treturn mux\n}\n\n// healthz returns the health status.\n// This is the liveness probe — it only checks that the process is alive.\n// Backend connectivity is checked by /readyz (the readiness probe).\nfunc (s *Server) healthz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n}\n\n// readyz returns the readiness status.\n// When the canary monitor is running and not disabled, its in-memory health\n// state is the sole signal — no backend call is made. When the canary is\n// disabled (ARMOR_CANARY_DISABLED=true), /readyz always returns 200 and the\n// liveness probe (/healthz) is the sole health guard. When the canary is not\n// configured, the manifest writer's last flush is used as the health signal.\nfunc (s *Server) readyz(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t// When canary is explicitly disabled, skip all backend checks.\n\tif s.canaryDisabled {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Canary monitor is authoritative when running.\n\tif s.canary != nil && s.canaryStarted {\n\t\tif !s.canary.IsHealthy() {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - canary check failed\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback: manifest writer's last successful delta flush.\n\t// A flush within the last 60 seconds indicates the service is healthy.\n\tconst flushThreshold = 60 * time.Second\n\tif s.manifestWriter != nil {\n\t\tlastFlush := s.manifestWriter.LastFlush()\n\t\tif !lastFlush.IsZero() && time.Since(lastFlush) < flushThreshold {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Ready\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// No recent flush — report unhealthy\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\tif lastFlush.IsZero() {\n\t\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - manifest writer has never flushed\"))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(w, \"Not ready - manifest writer last flush %v ago (threshold %v)\", time.Since(lastFlush).Round(time.Second), flushThreshold)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Neither canary nor manifest writer available — report unhealthy.\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\tw.Write([]byte(\"Not ready - no health signal available\"))\n}\n\n// verifyKey verifies the MEK is correct.\nfunc (s *Server) verifyKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif s.canary == nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n\tif status.DecryptVerified && status.HMACVerified {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"verified\",\"message\":\"MEK is correct\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unverified\",\"error\":\"canary check failed - MEK may be incorrect\"}`))\n}\n\n// rotateKey rotates the MEK.\nfunc (s *Server) rotateKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Read the new MEK from the request body\n\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer r.Body.Close()\n\n\t// The new MEK should be a 32-byte hex-encoded string (64 hex chars)\n\t// or raw 32 bytes\n\tvar newMEK []byte\n\tif len(body) == 64 {\n\t\t// Hex-encoded\n\t\tnewMEK, err = hex.DecodeString(string(body))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid hex-encoded MEK\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t} else if len(body) == 32 {\n\t\t// Raw bytes\n\t\tnewMEK = body\n\t} else {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid MEK length: expected 32 bytes or 64 hex chars, got %d\", len(body)), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create key rotator with the default key. Pass the manifest index so\n\t// rotateObject can skip per-object HeadObject calls when the entry is cached.\n\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n\trotator := NewKeyRotator(s.backend, s.config.Bucket, defaultKey.MEK, newMEK, s.manifest)\n\n\t// Perform rotation\n\tresult, err := rotator.Rotate(r.Context())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\":  \"failed\",\n\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n\t\t\t\"result\":  result,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Update the server's MEK on success\n\tif result.Status == \"completed\" {\n\t\tif err := s.keyManager.UpdateDefaultKey(newMEK); err != nil {\n\t\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"error\": err.Error(),\n\t\t\t}).Error(\"failed to update key manager after rotation\")\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Clear the metadata cache since DEKs are now wrapped with new MEK\n\t\ts.cache.Clear()\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n}\n\n// exportKey exports the current MEK.\nfunc (s *Server) exportKey(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"confirm\") != \"yes\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Must include ?confirm=yes to export key\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Export the default MEK as hex-encoded string\n\tdefaultKey := s.keyManager.DefaultKey()\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]string{\n\t\t\"mek\":   hex.EncodeToString(defaultKey.MEK),\n\t\t\"format\": \"hex\",\n\t\t\"warning\": \"This key provides access to all encrypted data. Store securely.\",\n\t})\n}\n\n// canaryHandler returns the canary status.\nfunc (s *Server) canaryHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tif s.canary == nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(`{\"status\":\"unknown\",\"error\":\"canary monitor not configured\"}`))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tstatus := s.canary.GetStatus()\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(status)\n}\n\n// audit returns the audit status.\nfunc (s *Server) audit(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create auditor and perform audit\n\tauditor := provenance.NewAuditor(s.backend, s.config.Bucket)\n\tresult, err := auditor.Audit(r.Context())\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"status\": \"error\",\n\t\t\t\"error\":   err.Error(),\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(result)\n}\n\n// wrapHandler wraps a handler with common middleware.\nfunc (s *Server) wrapHandler(h http.HandlerFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tstart := time.Now()\n\n\t\t// Track in-flight request\n\t\ts.requestTracker.Start()\n\t\tdefer s.requestTracker.End()\n\n\t\t// Log request\n\t\ts.logger.WithFields(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"method\": r.Method,\n\t\t\t\"path\":   r.URL.Path,\n\t\t}).Debug(\"incoming request\")\n\n\t\t// Add CORS headers for browser clients\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\", \"*\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\", \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\", \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n\n\t\t// Handle preflight\n\t\tif r.Method == http.MethodOptions {\n\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify auth for non-public endpoints\n\t\tif !s.isPublicPath(r.URL.Path) {\n\t\t\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif authErr, ok := err.(*AuthError); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n\t\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"auth\", 403)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// Check ACL for the request. For list operations the URL path has no\n\t\t\t// key component, so fall back to the ?prefix query param so that ACL\n\t\t\t// prefix restrictions are enforced correctly against the listed prefix.\n\t\t\tbucket, key := s.extractBucketAndKey(r)\n\t\t\tif key == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tkey = r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, key); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n\t\t\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(\"acl\", 403)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Decode aws-chunked body when MinIO streaming signature is used.\n\t\t// The seed signature in the Authorization header already authenticated the\n\t\t// request; we only need to strip the chunk framing to recover the payload.\n\t\tif r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Content-Sha256\") == \"STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD\" {\n\t\t\tif decoded := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Decoded-Content-Length\"); decoded != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tif n, err := strconv.ParseInt(decoded, 10, 64); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tr.ContentLength = n\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tr.Body = newAWSChunkedReader(r.Body)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Use a response writer wrapper to capture status code\n\t\trw := &responseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, statusCode: http.StatusOK}\n\n\t\th(rw, r)\n\n\t\t// Record metrics\n\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\t\ts.metrics.IncRequestsTotal(r.Method, rw.statusCode)\n\t\ts.metrics.RecordRequestDuration(r.Method, duration)\n\n\t\t// Log completed request\n\t\tfields := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"method\":      r.Method,\n\t\t\t\"path\":        r.URL.Path,\n\t\t\t\"status\":      rw.statusCode,\n\t\t\t\"duration_ms\": duration.Milliseconds(),\n\t\t}\n\t\tif rng := r.Header.Get(\"Range\"); rng != \"\" {\n\t\t\tfields[\"range\"] = rng\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.logger.WithFields(fields).Info(\"request completed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// responseWriter wraps http.ResponseWriter to capture the status code.\ntype responseWriter struct {\n\thttp.ResponseWriter\n\tstatusCode int\n}\n\n// WriteHeader captures the status code.\nfunc (rw *responseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {\n\trw.statusCode = code\n\trw.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)\n}\n\n// isPublicPath checks if a path is public (no auth required).\nfunc (s *Server) isPublicPath(path string) bool {\n\treturn path == \"/healthz\" || path == \"/readyz\"\n}\n\n// verifyAuthAndGetCredential validates AWS SigV4 authentication and returns the credential.\nfunc (s *Server) verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\t// Create auth with all credentials\n\tauth := NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(s.config.Credentials, s.config.B2Region)\n\n\t// Check for query-based auth (presigned URLs)\n\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\") != \"\" {\n\t\treturn auth.VerifyQueryAuth(r)\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for header-based auth\n\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Pass nil body — buildCanonicalRequest uses x-amz-content-sha256 header\n\t// when present (which covers all AWS SDK/CLI requests), falling back to\n\t// sha256(body) for requests without that header.\n\treturn auth.VerifyRequest(r, nil)\n}\n\n// extractBucketAndKey extracts bucket and key from the request URL.\nfunc (s *Server) extractBucketAndKey(r *http.Request) (bucket, key string) {\n\tpath := r.URL.Path\n\t// Remove leading slash\n\tpath = strings.TrimPrefix(path, \"/\")\n\n\t// Check for virtual-hosted-style (bucket in host)\n\t// For path-style: /bucket/key\n\tparts := strings.SplitN(path, \"/\", 2)\n\tif len(parts) >= 1 {\n\t\tbucket = parts[0]\n\t}\n\tif len(parts) >= 2 {\n\t\tkey = parts[1]\n\t\t// URL decode the key (DuckDB httpfs encodes special chars like = as %3D)\n\t\tif decoded, err := url.PathUnescape(key); err == nil {\n\t\t\tkey = decoded\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use configured bucket if empty\n\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n\t}\n\n\treturn bucket, key\n}\n\n// writeError writes an S3 error response.\nfunc (s *Server) writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string, statusCode int) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(statusCode)\n\tvar codeBuf, msgBuf bytes.Buffer\n\txml.EscapeText(&codeBuf, []byte(code))\n\txml.EscapeText(&msgBuf, []byte(message))\n\tfmt.Fprintf(w, `<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`+\"\\n<Error>\\n  <Code>%s</Code>\\n  <Message>%s</Message>\\n</Error>\",\n\t\tcodeBuf.String(), msgBuf.String())\n}\n\n// GenerateDEK generates a new DEK (exposed for handlers).\nfunc (s *Server) GenerateDEK() ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn crypto.GenerateDEK()\n}\n\n// GenerateIV generates a new IV (exposed for handlers).\nfunc (s *Server) GenerateIV() ([]byte, error) {\n\treturn crypto.GenerateIV()\n}\n\n// WaitForInFlightRequests waits for all in-flight requests to complete.\nfunc (s *Server) WaitForInFlightRequests() {\n\ts.requestTracker.Wait()\n}\n\n// InFlightRequestCount returns the current number of in-flight requests.\nfunc (s *Server) InFlightRequestCount() int64 {\n\treturn s.requestTracker.Count()\n}\n\n// handlePresign generates a pre-signed URL for sharing encrypted files.\n// POST /admin/presign\n// Body: {\"bucket\": \"my-bucket\", \"key\": \"path/to/file.parquet\", \"expires_in\": \"1h\", \"content_disposition\": \"attachment; filename=\\\"file.parquet\\\"\"}\nfunc (s *Server) handlePresign(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodPost {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify auth\n\tcred, err := s.verifyAuthAndGetCredential(r)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Return specific authentication error code and message\n\t\tif authErr, ok := err.(*AuthError); ok {\n\t\t\ts.writeError(w, authErr.Code, authErr.Message, 403)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Invalid credentials\", 403)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse request body\n\tvar req struct {\n\t\tBucket             string `json:\"bucket\"`\n\t\tKey                string `json:\"key\"`\n\t\tExpiresIn          string `json:\"expires_in\"`\n\t\tContentDisposition string `json:\"content_disposition\"`\n\t\tRange              string `json:\"range\"`\n\t}\n\n\tif err := json.NewDecoder(r.Body).Decode(&req); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid request body: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Use configured bucket if not specified\n\tbucket := req.Bucket\n\tif bucket == \"\" {\n\t\tbucket = s.config.Bucket\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate required fields\n\tif req.Key == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"key is required\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check ACL for the request\n\tif err := CheckACL(cred, bucket, req.Key); err != nil {\n\t\ts.writeError(w, \"AccessDenied\", \"Access Denied\", 403)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse expiration (default 1 hour)\n\texpiration := presign.DefaultExpiration\n\tif req.ExpiresIn != \"\" {\n\t\texpiration, err = presign.ParseExpiration(req.ExpiresIn)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid expires_in: %v\", err), http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Build options\n\tvar opts []presign.Option\n\tif req.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithContentDisposition(req.ContentDisposition))\n\t}\n\tif req.Range != \"\" {\n\t\topts = append(opts, presign.WithRange(req.Range))\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate pre-signed URL\n\tshareURL, err := s.presigner.GenerateURL(bucket, req.Key, expiration, opts...)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate URL: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Return response\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\tjson.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"url\":       shareURL,\n\t\t\"expires_in\": presign.FormatExpiration(expiration),\n\t\t\"expires_at\": time.Now().Add(expiration).UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),\n\t})\n}\n\n// handleShare serves decrypted content from a pre-signed URL token.\n// GET /share/<token>\nfunc (s *Server) handleShare(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif r.Method != http.MethodGet {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract token from path\n\ttokenStr := presign.ParseTokenFromPath(r.URL.Path)\n\tif tokenStr == \"\" {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Missing token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify token\n\ttoken, err := s.presigner.VerifyToken(tokenStr)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrExpiredToken) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Link expired\", http.StatusGone)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif errors.Is(err, presign.ErrInvalidSignature) {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid link\", http.StatusForbidden)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid token\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get object metadata\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tinfo, err := s.backend.Head(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), http.StatusNotFound)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if object is ARMOR-encrypted\n\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n\t\t// Serve non-ARMOR objects directly (passthrough)\n\t\tbody, _, err := s.backend.Get(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer body.Close()\n\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", info.Size))\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n\t\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n\t\t}\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to parse object metadata\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the MEK for this object\n\tmek, err := s.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to get decryption key\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Unwrap DEK\n\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to unwrap DEK\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Create decryptor\n\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Failed to create decryptor\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", armorMeta.PlaintextSize))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n\tif token.ContentDisposition != \"\" {\n\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Disposition\", token.ContentDisposition)\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle range request if specified in token or header\n\trangeHeader := token.Range\n\tif rangeHeader == \"\" {\n\t\trangeHeader = r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n\t}\n\n\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n\t\ts.handleShareRangeRequest(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta, rangeHeader)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Full object download\n\ts.handleShareFullObject(w, r, token, decryptor, armorMeta)\n}\n\n// handleShareFullObject handles full object downloads for share endpoint.\nfunc (s *Server) handleShareFullObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\n\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(armorMeta.PlaintextSize, blockSize))\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Calculate offsets\n\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n\n\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table\n\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to fetch HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\thmacTable, err := io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\thmacBody.Close()\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// 2. Stream data from Cloudflare\n\tstreamSize := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n\tdataBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(ctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, 0, streamSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n\n\t// 3. Read and discard header\n\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// 4. Write status before streaming\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\n\t// 5. Stream decrypt\n\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n\n\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif n == 0 {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n\n\t\t// Verify HMAC\n\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n\n\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n\n\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Decrypt\n\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n\n\t\t// Write to client\n\t\tw.Write(decrypted)\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleShareRangeRequest handles range requests for share endpoint.\nfunc (s *Server) handleShareRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, token *presign.Token, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, rangeHeader string) {\n\tctx := r.Context()\n\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n\n\t// Parse range header\n\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Invalid range\", http.StatusBadRequest)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel\n\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n\n\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n\n\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\tencryptedBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n\n\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tg.Go(func() error {\n\t\thmacBody, err := s.backend.GetRange(gctx, token.Bucket, token.Key, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n\n\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n\n\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Decrypt range\n\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Set response headers\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", len(plaintext)))\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n\tw.Write(plaintext)\n}\n\nfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\nfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n\treturn counter\n}\n\n// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\nfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n\n\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n\t}\n\n\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t} else {\n\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t}\n\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start || end >= totalSize {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn start, end, nil\n}\n\n// handleB2Keys handles B2 application key management.\n// GET: List keys\n// POST: Create a new key\nfunc (s *Server) handleB2Keys(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tif s.b2keys == nil {\n\t\thttp.Error(w, `{\"error\":\"B2 key management not available - check B2 credentials\"}`, http.StatusServiceUnavailable)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tswitch r.Method {\n\tcase http.MethodGet:\n\t\ts.listB2Keys(w, r)\n\tcase http.MethodPost:\n\t\ts.createB2Key(w, r)\n\tdefault:\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"Method not allowed\", http.StatusMethodNotAllowed)\n\t}\n}\n\n// listB2Keys lists B2 application keys.\nfunc (s *Server) listB2Keys(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\tcount := 100\n\tif c SigV4Auth handles AWS Signature Version 4 authentication.\n19\ttype SigV4Auth struct {\n20\t\tcredentials map[string]*config.Credential // access key -> credential\n21\t\tregion      string\n22\t\tservice     string\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// NewSigV4Auth creates a new SigV4 authenticator with a single credential.\n26\tfunc NewSigV4Auth(accessKey, secretKey, region string) *SigV4Auth nil, // nil means full access\n33\t\t\t\t},\n34\t\t\t},\n35\t\t\tregion:  region,\n36\t\t\tservice: \"s3\",\n37\t\t}\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t// NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials creates a SigV4 authenticator with multiple credentials.\n41\tfunc NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(credentials map[string]*config.Credential, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n42\t\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n43\t\t\tcredentials: credentials,\n44\t\t\tregion:      region,\n45\t\t\tservice:     \"s3\",\n46\t\t}\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// AuthHeader represents parsed SigV4 Authorization header components.\n50\ttype AuthHeader struct {\n51\t\tAlgorithm     string\n52\t\tAccessKey     string\n53\t\tCredentialDate string\n54\t\tRegion        string\n55\t\tService       string\n56\t\tSignedHeaders []string\n57\t\tSignature     string\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t// ParseAuthHeader parses the AWS SigV4 Authorization header.\n61\t// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=accesskey/date/region/service/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\n62\tfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n63\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n64\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAlgorithm\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\tauth = strings.TrimPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \")\n68\t\t// Split by \",\" to handle both regular (\", \") and streaming (\",\") auth header formats.\n69\t\trawParts := strings.Split(auth, \",\")\n70\t\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(rawParts))\n71\t\tfor _, p := range rawParts {\n72\t\t\tif t := strings.TrimSpace(p); t != \"\" {\n73\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, t)\n74\t\t\t}\n75\t\t}\n76\t\n77\t\tresult := &AuthHeader{\n78\t\t\tAlgorithm: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n82\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n83\t\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n84\t\t\t\tcontinue\n85\t\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\t\tswitch kv[0] {\n88\t\t\tcase \"Credential\":\n89\t\t\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(kv[1], \"/\")\n90\t\t\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n91\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n92\t\t\t\t}\n93\t\t\t\tresult.AccessKey = credParts[0]\n94\t\t\t\tresult.CredentialDate = credParts[1]\n95\t\t\t\tresult.Region = credParts[2]\n96\t\t\t\tresult.Service = credParts[3]\n97\t\t\t\t// credParts[4] should be \"aws4_request\"\n98\t\n99\t\t\tcase \"SignedHeaders\":\n100\t\t\t\tresult.SignedHeaders = strings.Split(kv[1], \";\")\n101\t\n102\t\t\tcase \"Signature\":\n103\t\t\t\tresult.Signature = kv[1]\n104\t\t\t}\n105\t\t}\n106\t\n107\t\tif result.AccessKey == \"\" || len(result.SignedHeaders) == 0 || result.Signature == \"\" {\n108\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n109\t\t}\n110\t\n111\t\treturn result, nil\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\t// VerifyRequest verifies the SigV4 signature on an HTTP request.\n115\t// Returns the credential if verification succeeds, or an error if it fails.\n116\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n117\t\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n118\t\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n119\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n123\t\tif err != nil {\n124\t\t\treturn nil, err\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Look up credential by access key\n128\t\tcred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n129\t\tif !exists {\n130\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n131\t\t}\n132\t\n133\t\t// Get the timestamp from headers\n134\t\tamzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n135\t\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n136\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n137\t\t}\n138\t\n139\t\t// Parse and verify timestamp is within 15 minutes\n140\t\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n141\t\tif err != nil {\n142\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n143\t\t}\n144\t\n145\t\tif diff := time.Since(requestTime); diff < -15*time.Minute || diff > 15*time.Minute {\n146\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n147\t\t}\n148\t\n149\t\t// Build canonical request\n150\t\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n151\t\n152\t\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n153\t\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n154\t\n155\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n156\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n157\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n158\t\n159\t\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n160\t\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n161\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n162\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\treturn cred, nil\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\n168\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n169\t\t// 1. HTTP method\n170\t\tmethod := r.Method\n171\t\n172\t\t// 2. Canonical URI (URL-encoded path)\n173\t\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n174\t\tif path == \"\" {\n175\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n176\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\t// 3. Canonical query string\n179\t\tquery := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(r.URL.Query())\n180\t\n181\t\t// 4. Canonical headers\n182\t\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n183\t\n184\t\t// 5. Signed headers\n185\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n186\t\n187\t\t// 6. Hashed payload\n188\t\t// Prefer the x-amz-content-sha256 header when present — the client already\n189\t\t// computed and signed this value, so we don't need to read the body (which\n190\t\t// would consume it before the handler runs).\n191\t\tpayloadHash := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n192\t\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n193\t\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Sum(body)\n194\t\t}\n195\t\n196\t\t// Combine\n197\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n198\t\t\tmethod,\n199\t\t\tpath,\n200\t\t\tquery,\n201\t\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n202\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n203\t\t\tpayloadHash,\n204\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\n208\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n209\t\tif len(query) == 0 {\n210\t\t\treturn \"\"\n211\t\t}\n212\t\n213\t\t// Sort keys\n214\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n215\t\tfor k := range query {\n216\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n217\t\t}\n218\t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n219\t\n220\t\tvar parts []string\n221\t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n222\t\t\t// Sort values for each key\n223\t\t\tvalues := query[k]\n224\t\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n225\t\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n226\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// buildCanonicalHeaders builds the canonical headers string.\n234\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalHeaders(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n235\t\tvar lines []string\n236\t\n237\t\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n238\t\t\t// Handle host specially - use the actual request host\n239\t\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n240\t\t\t\thost := r.Host\n241\t\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n242\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n243\t\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n244\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n246\t\t\t\tcontinue\n247\t\t\t}\n248\t\n249\t\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n250\t\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n251\t\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n252\t\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n253\t\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n254\t\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n255\t\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n256\t\t\t\t\t}\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t\tcontinue\n259\t\t\t}\n260\t\n261\t\t\t// Trim leading/trailing whitespace and collapse multiple spaces\n262\t\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n263\t\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n264\t\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n265\t\t\t}\n266\t\t\tlines = append(lines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n267\t\t}\n268\t\n269\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// buildStringToSign builds the string to sign per AWS spec.\n273\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest string) string {\n274\t\tcredentialScope := credentialDate + \"/\" + region + \"/\" + a.service + \"/aws4_request\"\n275\t\n276\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n277\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n278\t\t\tamzDate,\n279\t\t\tcredentialScope,\n280\t\t\tsha256Hex([]byte(canonicalRequest)),\n281\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t// getSigningKeyForCredential derives the signing key for the given credential, date, and region.\n285\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) getSigningKeyForCredential(cred *config.Credential, date, region string) []byte {\n286\t\tkDate := a.hmacSHA256([]byte(\"AWS4\"+cred.SecretKey), date)\n287\t\tkRegion := a.hmacSHA256(kDate, region)\n288\t\tkService := a.hmacSHA256(kRegion, a.service)\n289\t\tkSigning := a.hmacSHA256(kService, \"aws4_request\")\n290\t\treturn kSigning\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t// CheckACL verifies that the credential is allowed to access the given bucket and key.\n294\t// If the credential has no ACLs (nil), it has full access.\n295\tfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n296\t\t// No ACLs means full access\n297\t\tif len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n298\t\t\treturn nil\n299\t\t}\n300\t\n301\t\tfor _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n302\t\t\t// Check bucket match\n303\t\t\tif acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n304\t\t\t\tcontinue\n305\t\t\t}\n306\t\n307\t\t\t// Check prefix match\n308\t\t\tif acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n309\t\t\t\t// Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n310\t\t\t\treturn nil\n311\t\t\t}\n312\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n313\t\t\t\treturn nil\n314\t\t\t}\n315\t\t}\n316\t\n317\t\treturn ErrAccessDenied\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\t// hmacSHA256 computes HMAC-SHA256.\n321\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) hmacSHA256(key []byte, data string) []byte {\n322\t\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)\n323\t\th.Write([]byte(data))\n324\t\treturn h.Sum(nil)\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// sha256Sum computes SHA256 hash as hex string.\n328\tfunc sha256Sum(data []byte) string {\n329\t\treturn sha256Hex(data)\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\t// sha256Hex computes SHA256 hash and returns hex encoding.\n333\tfunc sha256Hex(data []byte) string {\n334\t\thash := sha256.Sum256(data)\n335\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// Authentication errors\n339\tvar (\n340\t\tErrMissingAuthHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", Message: \"Missing Authentication Token\"}\n341\t\tErrInvalidAlgorithm    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAlgorithm\", Message: \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\"}\n342\t\tErrInvalidCredential   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidCredential\", Message: \"Invalid credential format\"}\n343\t\tErrMissingFields       = &AuthError{Code: \"IncompleteSignature\", Message: \"Authorization header is missing required fields\"}\n344\t\tErrInvalidAccessKey    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", Message: \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\"}\n345\t\tErrMissingDateHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingDateHeader\", Message: \"Missing X-Amz-Date Package server implements the ARMOR S3-compatible HTTP server.\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"crypto/hmac\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n)\n\n// SigV4Auth handles AWS Signature Version 4 authentication.\ntype SigV4Auth struct {\n\tcredentials map[string]*config.Credential // access key -> credential\n\tregion      string\n\tservice     string\n}\n\n// NewSigV4Auth creates a new SigV4 authenticator with a single credential.\nfunc NewSigV4Auth(accessKey, secretKey, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n\t\tcredentials: map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\taccessKey: {\n\t\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n\t\t\t\tSecretKey: secretKey,\n\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil, // nil means full access\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tregion:  region,\n\t\tservice: \"s3\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials creates a SigV4 authenticator with multiple credentials.\nfunc NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(credentials map[string]*config.Credential, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n\t\tcredentials: credentials,\n\t\tregion:      region,\n\t\tservice:     \"s3\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// AuthHeader represents parsed SigV4 Authorization header components.\ntype AuthHeader struct {\n\tAlgorithm     string\n\tAccessKey     string\n\tCredentialDate string\n\tRegion        string\n\tService       string\n\tSignedHeaders []string\n\tSignature     string\n}\n\n// ParseAuthHeader parses the AWS SigV4 Authorization header.\n// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=accesskey/date/region/service/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\nfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAlgorithm\n\t}\n\n\tauth = strings.TrimPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \")\n\t// Split by \",\" to handle both regular (\", \") and streaming (\",\") auth header formats.\n\trawParts := strings.Split(auth, \",\")\n\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(rawParts))\n\tfor _, p := range rawParts {\n\t\tif t := strings.TrimSpace(p); t != \"\" {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &AuthHeader{\n\t\tAlgorithm: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch kv[0] {\n\t\tcase \"Credential\":\n\t\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(kv[1], \"/\")\n\t\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.AccessKey = credParts[0]\n\t\t\tresult.CredentialDate = credParts[1]\n\t\t\tresult.Region = credParts[2]\n\t\t\tresult.Service = credParts[3]\n\t\t\t// credParts[4] should be \"aws4_request\"\n\n\t\tcase \"SignedHeaders\":\n\t\t\tresult.SignedHeaders = strings.Split(kv[1], \";\")\n\n\t\tcase \"Signature\":\n\t\t\tresult.Signature = kv[1]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif result.AccessKey == \"\" || len(result.SignedHeaders) == 0 || result.Signature == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// VerifyRequest verifies the SigV4 signature on an HTTP request.\n// Returns the credential if verification succeeds, or an error if it fails.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up credential by access key\n\tcred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the timestamp from headers\n\tamzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and verify timestamp is within 15 minutes\n\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n\t}\n\n\tif diff := time.Since(requestTime); diff < -15*time.Minute || diff > 15*time.Minute {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n\t}\n\n\t// Build canonical request\n\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n\n\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n\n\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n\n\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n\t}\n\n\treturn cred, nil\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n\t// 1. HTTP method\n\tmethod := r.Method\n\n\t// 2. Canonical URI (URL-encoded path)\n\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\tpath = \"/\"\n\t}\n\n\t// 3. Canonical query string\n\tquery := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(r.URL.Query())\n\n\t// 4. Canonical headers\n\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n\n\t// 5. Signed headers\n\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n\n\t// 6. Hashed payload\n\t// Prefer the x-amz-content-sha256 header when present — the client already\n\t// computed and signed this value, so we don't need to read the body (which\n\t// would consume it before the handler runs).\n\tpayloadHash := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Sum(body)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine\n\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n\t\tmethod,\n\t\tpath,\n\t\tquery,\n\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n\t\tpayloadHash,\n\t}, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n\tif len(query) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort keys\n\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n\tfor k := range query {\n\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n\t}\n\tsort.Strings(keys)\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor _, k := range keys {\n\t\t// Sort values for each key\n\t\tvalues := query[k]\n\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalHeaders builds the canonical headers string.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalHeaders(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n\t\t// Handle host specially - use the actual request host\n\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n\t\t\thost := r.Host\n\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Trim leading/trailing whitespace and collapse multiple spaces\n\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n}\n\n// buildStringToSign builds the string to sign per AWS spec.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest string) string {\n\tcredentialScope := credentialDate + \"/\" + region + \"/\" + a.service + \"/aws4_request\"\n\n\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\tamzDate,\n\t\tcredentialScope,\n\t\tsha256Hex([]byte(canonicalRequest)),\n\t}, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// getSigningKeyForCredential derives the signing key for the given credential, date, and region.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) getSigningKeyForCredential(cred *config.Credential, date, region string) []byte {\n\tkDate := a.hmacSHA256([]byte(\"AWS4\"+cred.SecretKey), date)\n\tkRegion := a.hmacSHA256(kDate, region)\n\tkService := a.hmacSHA256(kRegion, a.service)\n\tkSigning := a.hmacSHA256(kService, \"aws4_request\")\n\treturn kSigning\n}\n\n// CheckACL verifies that the credential is allowed to access the given bucket and key.\n// If the credential has no ACLs (nil), it has full access.\nfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n\t// No ACLs means full access\n\tif len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n\t\t// Check bucket match\n\t\tif acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check prefix match\n\t\tif acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ErrAccessDenied\n}\n\n// hmacSHA256 computes HMAC-SHA256.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) hmacSHA256(key []byte, data string) []byte {\n\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)\n\th.Write([]byte(data))\n\treturn h.Sum(nil)\n}\n\n// sha256Sum computes SHA256 hash as hex string.\nfunc sha256Sum(data []byte) string {\n\treturn sha256Hex(data)\n}\n\n// sha256Hex computes SHA256 hash and returns hex encoding.\nfunc sha256Hex(data []byte) string {\n\thash := sha256.Sum256(data)\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n}\n\n// Authentication errors\nvar (\n\tErrMissingAuthHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", Message: \"Missing Authentication Token\"}\n\tErrInvalidAlgorithm    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAlgorithm\", Message: \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\"}\n\tErrInvalidCredential   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidCredential\", Message: \"Invalid credential format\"}\n\tErrMissingFields       = &AuthError{Code: \"IncompleteSignature\", Message: \"Authorization header is missing required fields\"}\n\tErrInvalidAccessKey    = time.Duration(expiresSec)*time.Second {\n\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// For query auth, the body is typically empty for GET requests\n\tbody := []byte{}\n\n\t// Build claimed\n\tcredentialDate := credParts[1]\n\tclientRegion := credParts[2]\n\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, clientRegion, canonicalRequest)\n\n\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, clientRegion)\n\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n\n\tif calculatedSig != signature {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n\t}\n\n\treturn cred, nil\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalQueryRequest builds canonical request for query-based auth.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte, query url.Values) string {\n\t// Create a copy of Authentication Errors (HTTP 403 Forbidden)\n\nAuthentication errors occur when request credentials cannot be verified.\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| `MissingAuthenticationToken` | Missing Authentication Token | Authorization header is missing | ✅ Yes |\n| `InvalidAccessKeyId` | The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist | Provided access key not found in credentials | ✅ Yes |\n| `SignatureDoesNotMatch` | The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided | Calculated signature doesn't match provided signature | ✅ Yes |\n| `InvalidAlgorithm` | Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported | Authorization header doesn't start with \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\" | ✅ Yes |\n| `IncompleteSignature` | Authorization header is missing required fields | Auth header missing Credential, SignedHeaders, or Signature | ✅ Yes |\n| `MissingDateHeader` | Missing X-Amz-Date header | X-Amz-Date header not provided | ✅ Yes |\n| `InvalidDateFormat` | Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header | Date format not valid \"20060102T150405Z\" | ⚠️ Limited |\n| `RequestExpired` | Request has expired | Request timestamp outside 15-minute window | ✅ Yes |\n| `InvalidCredential` | Invalid credential format | Credential doesn't have 5 parts (access/date/region/service/aws4_request) | ⚠️ Limited |\n\n**Test Files:**\n- `internal/server/error_response_verification_test.go` - Comprehensive verification\n- `internal/server/invalid_credential_integration_test.go` - Integration tests\n- `internal/server/auth_test.go` - Unit tests\n\n### 2. Authorization Errors (HTTP 403 Forbidden)\n\nAuthorization errors occur when authenticated credentials don't have permission to access a resource.\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| `AccessDenied` | Access Denied | ACL check fails (no matching prefix/bucket in ACL) | ✅ Yes |\n| `AccessDenied` | Invalid credentials | Generic auth failure (fallback error) | ✅ Yes |\n| `AccessDenied` | Access to .armor/ reserved namespace is denied | Attempt to access any key under `.armor/` prefix | ✅ Yes |\n\n**Test Files:**\n- `internal/server/error_response_verification_test.go`\n- `internal/server/armor_namespace_test.go` - Namespace protection tests\n\n### 3. Validation Errors (HTTP 400 Bad Request)\n\nValidation errors occur when request parameters or body are malformed or invalid.\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| `InvalidRequest` | Unsupported POST operation | POST without recognized query params (?uploads, ?uploadId, ?delete) | ⚠️ Limited |\n| `InvalidRequest` | Missing x-amz-copy-source header | CopyObject without copy source | ❌ No |\n| `InvalidCopySource` | Invalid copy source format | Malformed copy source header | ❌ No |\n| `InvalidRequest` | Missing partNumber | UploadPart without partNumber | ❌ No |\n| `InvalidRequest` | Invalid partNumber | partNumber out of range (1-10000) | ❌ No |\n| `MalformedXML` | Failed to parse XML | XML parsing failure in DeleteObjects/CompleteMultipartUpload | ❌ No |\n| `MalformedXML` | No objects specified for deletion | DeleteObjects with empty object list | ❌ No |\n| `InvalidRequest` | No parts specified | CompleteMultipartUpload with empty parts list | ❌ No |\n| `InvalidRange` | Invalid range: [error details] | Range header parsing failure | ⚠️ Limited |\n\n**Test Files:**\n- Limited coverage - most validation errors lack dedicated tests\n\n### 4. Not Found Errors (HTTP 404 Not Found)\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| `NoSuchKey` | Object not found: [error details] | GetObject/HeadObject/CopyObject source doesn't exist | ✅ Yes |\n| `NoSuchBucket` | Bucket not found: [error details] | HeadBucket operation on non-existent bucket | ⚠️ Limited |\n| `NoSuchUpload` | Multipart upload not found: [error details] | UploadPart/CompleteMultipartUpload/AbortMultipartUpload/ListParts on invalid uploadId | ⚠️ Limited |\n\n**Test Files:**\n- Limited coverage - mostly covered by integration tests with real backend\n\n### 5. Internal Errors (HTTP 500 Internal Server Error)\n\nInternal errors indicate server-side failures during request processing.\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage Method Not Allowed (HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed)\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| `MethodNotAllowed` | Method X not allowed | Unsupported HTTP method for endpoint | ❌ No |\n\n**Test Coverage:** ❌ No dedicated tests\n\n### 8. Not Modified (HTTP 304 Not Modified)\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| (None - special status) | (None) | If-None-Match/If-Modified-Since condition succeeds | ⚠️ Limited |\n\n**Note:** HTTP 304 responses don't include an error body - they only include headers\n\n## Summary Statistics\n\n| Category | Error Codes | Test Coverage |\n|----------|--------------|---------------|\n| Authentication | 9 | ✅ 78% (7/9) |\n| Authorization | 3 | ✅ 100% (3/3) |\n| Validation | 9 | ⚠️ 22% (2/9) |\n| Not Found | 3 | ⚠️ 33% (1/3) |\n| Internal | 50+ | ❌ 0% (0/50+) |\n| Precondition | 1 | ⚠️ Limited |\n| Method Not Allowed | 1 | ❌ 0% |\n| **Total** | **76+** | **~15%** |\n\n**Overall Assessment:**\n- **Strong Coverage:** Authentication and authorization errors are well-tested\n- **Weak Coverage:** Validation errors have limited test coverage\n- **Missing Coverage:** Internal errors and method-based errors lack dedicated tests\n- **Risk:** Most error scenarios lack verification that they return correct HTTP status codes and messages\n\n## Recommendations\n\n1. **Add validation error tests** - Create dedicated tests for all validation error scenarios\n2. **Add method-based tests** - Test that unsupported HTTP methods return 405\n3. **Add not-found tests** - Test NoSuchKey and NoSuchBucket scenarios with real backend\n4. **Document internal error paths** - Not Modified (HTTP 304 Not Modified)\n\n| Error Code | Message | Trigger | Test Coverage |\n|------------|---------|---------|---------------|\n| (None - special status) | (None) | If-None-Match/If-Modified-Since condition succeeds | ⚠️ Limited |\n\n**Note:** HTTP 304 responses don't include an error body - they only include headers\n\n## Summary Statistics\n\n| Category | Error Codes | Test Coverage |\n|----------|--------------|---------------|\n| Authentication | 9 | ✅ 78% (7/9) |\n| Authorization | 3 | ✅ 100% (3/3) |\n| Validation | 9 | ⚠️ 22% (2/9) |\n| Not Found | 3 | ⚠️ 33% (1/3) |\n| Internal | 50+ | ❌ 0% (0/50+) |\n| Precondition | 1 | ⚠️ Limited |\n| Method Not Allowed | 1 | ❌ 0% |\n| **Total** | **76+** | **~15%** |\n\n**Overall Assessment:**\n- **Strong Coverage:** Authentication and authorization errors are well-tested\n- **Weak Coverage:** Validation errors have limited test coverage\n- **Missing Coverage:** Internal errors and method-based errors lack dedicated tests\n- **Risk:** Most error scenarios lack verification that they return correct HTTP status codes and messages\n\n## Recommendations\n\n1. **Add validation error tests** - Create dedicated tests for all validation error scenarios\n2. **Add method-based tests** - Test that unsupported HTTP methods return 405\n3. **Add not-found tests** - Test NoSuchKey and NoSuchBucket scenarios with real backend\n4. **Document internal error paths** - While deterministic testing is difficult, document the error paths\n5. **Add conditional request tests** - Comprehensive testing of If-Match/If-None-Match headers\n6. **Performance tests** - Verify all error responses complete within 100ms target\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n- [Error Response Quality and Performance](/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/bf-5vghg8-error-response-quality-and-performance.md)\n- [ARMOR Architecture](/home/coding/ARMOR/README.md)\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}
